issue_title: [Bug]: Mobile UI: navigation bar overflows viewport, notification bell clipped on right edge
issue_body: ### What is your BTCPay version? © BTCPay Server v2.3.5+a13603a83 ### How did you deploy BTCPay Server? Docker ### What happened? ## Description After a recent update, the top navigation bar on mobile overflows the viewport width. The rightmost icon (notification bell) is partially clipped and barely visible at the screen edge. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Open a BTCPay Server instance on a mobile browser (Android / Chrome) 2. Navigate to a store dashboard 3. Observe the top navigation bar ## Expected Behavior All header elements (hamburger menu, logo, store selector, notification bell) fit within the mobile viewport and are fully visible and tappable. ## Actual Behavior The navigation bar content exceeds the viewport width. The notification bell icon on the far right is cut off, with only a sliver visible at the screen edge. This suggests a horizontal overflow issue in the header layout. ## Screenshot ## Environment - **Device:** Android smartphone - **Browser:** Google Chrome (mobile) - **BTCPay Server version:** Latest (self-hosted) ### How did you encounter this bug? Described above. ### Relevant log output ```shell ``` ### What browser do you use? Chromium based browser on a pixel 9 pro XL ### Additional information _No response_ ### Are you sure this is a bug report? - [x] I confirm this is a bug report

We have already this feature? You can set a minimum price for an item in the PoS. This give the user the ability to pay more.

@NicolasDorier the current solution is to disable Custom Payments and create a product with min 1 sat (see [donations.davidcoen.it](url) and https://chat.btcpayserver.org/btcpayserver/pl/tzr9kdmjx38wff8c493ud918jc). It's not super intuitive, so maybe if Custom Payments is enable there should be a trigger for 1 sat min payment. it might be considered as a bug because it can be used for spam attacks (I'm not sure what's the goal and if the server gives some info about routing / connected nodes if a 0 sat invoice is created). https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9ee9b5f0-dce6-4ae3-966b-b1be953b9141

Workaround works for [me](https://kitty.petzsch.eu/).

But have to agree: It's not super intuitive and would probably not work for the keypad usecase.
Note that having a minimum won't solve the gap limit issue. When an invoice is created, an address is consumed.

issue_title: Bug: Unable to copy fiat amount in Invoice Checkout
issue_body: Clicking on the fiat amount copy `NaN` in the clipboard (Total Fiat and Exchange Rate)
issue_comments: To note, it should just copy the value `99.00` and not `$99.00`. I believe we've had that issue in the past, but in a different view.
Hi , I’d like to work on this issue, but I’m having trouble locating the relevant file or code section. Could you please point me in the right direction or suggest where to start?

Hi! I'd like to work on this issue. 

I've identified the problem: the `asNumber()` function in the Vue binding returns a number, but the `data-clipboard` attribute expects a string value. 
This causes the clipboard to copy `NaN` instead of the actual amount.

I'll submit a PR to fix this by ensuring the value is properly converted to a string for the clipboard attribute.

Reverted on https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/commit/afcad151f2b6a72913a12f4e28007bfdee109386

The commit wasn't working for every currency (notably, JPY). It is better to copy NaN, where the user will notice that something is wrong, than copying a wrong amount that can wreck accounting if the user doesn't notice the amount isn't correct.

issue_title: Allow Fallback Exchange Rate to Be Enabled via API
issue_body:
In 2.1.2 we've added invoice rate fallback https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/issues/5622 https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/pull/6705 however, we have a request from a user wanting this option via the API, which makes sense. ### Discussed in https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/discussions/6806
Originally posted by **nemanja-z** June 25, 2025 The PUT /api/v1/stores/{storeId}/rates/configuration/{rateSource} endpoint currently supports setting the exchange rate provider for either primary or fallback sources. However, it only works for fallback if that option has already been enabled manually through the UI. As of now, there doesn’t seem to be an API method to enable the fallback exchange rate feature itself. This gap makes it difficult to fully automate store rate configuration via API. It would be helpful to expose an API endpoint (or enhance the existing one) to programmatically enable or toggle the fallback exchange rate option.
issue_comments: @pavlenex I can work on this one
This is a bug. @Abhijay007 go for it.

issue_title: [Bug]: 0 amount invoices no longer work as free items in POS, but generate any amount
issue_body: ### What is your BTCPay version? v2.1.2+c6f960cef ### How did you deploy BTCPay Server? Docker ### What happened? Since updating to 2.1.2 it seems that we broke how we handle invoice with the amount 0 in the POS. To replicate go to https://pay.pavlenex.com/apps/2SfptYNoGkDKoc5Sc5C2s6jXpQWD/pos 1. Invoice with amount = 0 is free item and it is automatically settled 2. Invoice with the amount null is any amount, or topup invoice, which accepts any amount To replicate you can even generate the invoice manually with amount=0 and it will settle. The only issue is that it seems our recent changes around POS likely made a breaking change there, leading all freebie items in POS to become donation ware (any amount). We even apply `free tag` ### How did you encounter this bug? 1. Create a POS item with amount = 0 2. Version 2.1.1 3. Checkout and see any amount instead of instantly settled. ### Relevant log output ```shell { "id": "free-registration", "title": "Free Registration (You already have BTCPay Merch)", "disabled": false, "description": "", "categories": null, "image": "", "priceType": "Fixed", "price": "0", "buyButtonText": "Free registration", "inventory": 2, "taxRate": null }, ``` ### What browser do you use? _No response_ ### Additional information _No response_ ### Are you sure this is a bug report? - [x] I confirm this is a bug report

issue_title: No Error in Checkout Experience when limit is exceeded
issue_body: I set the limit in the Checkout Experience for the BTC-LN payment method: Now when I do a payment exceeding this limit, no error is shown, the page is just refreshed with Empty Cart. This also applies for Keypad PoS. This is a bad UX and at least an error should be shown. Even better, the Pay button should be disabled with an explanation why is it disabled.
issue_comments:
I did think we at least showed an error if it exceeded the limit? I guess not - agree with all of the above - we'll address.

Thanks for the report!
I'll investiagte and fix :)

We show errors for that case, though they can be improved. That's what I did in #6639.

However, we still need to find out, why they aren't shown for you, @prusnak. Can you let me know the URL for your POS app, so that I can reproduce it on your instance? You can also reach out on Mattermost.

> Can you let me know the URL for your POS app,

https://donate.vexl.foundation - the limit is 1000 EUR
Ok, I can reproduce it now. The issue is the in-between redirect, because the app is mapped to a domain. The additional redirect removes the temporary messages — will fix.

issue_title: [Bug]: Dashboard does not show fiat balance with LND backend
issue_body:
### What is your BTCPay version?

BTCPay Server v2.0.4+6f9e0dca5

### How did you deploy BTCPay Server?

Docker

### What happened?

On the dashboard in the Lightning Balance section, by default it shows the BTC value of the BTC in channels and on-chain. In the top right there is a radio button so show the fiat value of those BTC, with the LND backend nothing happens when you click the fiat currency.

### How did you encounter this bug?

I have multiple stores, the store with the LND backend (REST) is the only one where this is not working.

I tried:
- Setting the default fiat currency to USD (I had it on EUR)
- Completely reinstalling BTCPayServer 2.0.4, this also happens on a fresh instance

With a Blink wallet backend this works fine (but there it only shows BTC in Channels)
With Bree  Greenlight backend this works fine

With Blink and Breez it also shows a chart, with LND backend it does not.

### Relevant log output

```shell
Unfortunately there are no log entries in both the BTCPayServer docker container, and also not in the Web Developer Tools Console.
```


### What browser do you use?

Brave Version 1.73.101 Chromium: 131.0.6778.139 (Official Build) (arm64)

### Additional information

Using LND v0.18.3-beta

### Are you sure this is a bug report?

- [X] I confirm this is a bug report
issue_comments:
Can you please check the browser console for errors? 

@pavlenex I think this is the same problem you reported with #6458.

> Can you please check the browser console for errors?

I did, that's what I meant with "Web Developer Tools Console" but unfortunately nothing there (except the blazor info messages)

In the browser console, do you see network request for `/api/rates` when switching the rate? If so, are these requests successful and what does the content look like?

What is the store's default fiat currency?

> In the browser console, do you see network request for `/api/rates` when switching the rate? If so, are these requests successful and what does the content look like?

When I do this at the "Lightning Balance" card, no. There is also a "Wallet Balance" card above it that is working and there I indeed see calls to `/api/rates` 

The store default fiat currency is EUR, but I set it to USD and that does not make a difference (also tried setting up a whole new BTCPayServer instance with the same LND node as backend, with USD as default fiat currency and that also does not make a difference).

Might be related: when comparing the XHR reponse between the blink backend and the LND REST backend, with the LND REST backend the `<template></template>` tag section with [JSON in it](https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/blob/master/BTCPayServer/Components/StoreLightningBalance/Default.cshtml#L159)  is absent

issue_title: API error when adding a non existing user to a store not clear
issue_body:
I was trying to add a user to a store via the API (/api/v1/stores/{storeId}/users). The following error returned:
```
{"code":"duplicate-store-user-role","message":"The user is already added to the store"}
```

But the user wasn't added to the store yet. After some investigation it appeared the user wasn't existing anymore in my test environment. So the error message should be different I guess.

If I understand the following code correctly, there is no check if the user in the request does exist:
https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/blob/c6fc0302d2df2b61853ff4702a1c2e28a6497d67/BTCPayServer/Controllers/GreenField/GreenfieldStoreUsersController.cs#L84

This exception is showing up even when the test succeed.
https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/pull/6416#pullrequestreview-2450066029

issue_title: [Bug]: Payment History in payment requests is invisible for `pending payment` and invalid invoices shown as pending
issue_body: ### What is your BTCPay version? 2.0.3 ### How did you deploy BTCPay Server? Docker ### What happened? Due to our recent changes in the logic https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/pull/6381 we overlooked certain scenarios that is leading to UX confusion. In this concrete scenario, a payment request invoice is `invalid` but the payment request UI shows it as `Pending`. What is even more confusing that this is that the `Payment history` below doesn't even recognize that the payment has been made, leading to the confusion on the end of the user who paid, thinking he didn't make a payment to an adequate address. Expected behaviour: 1. Invalid invoice should be shown as invalid state in the payment request (lower prio) 2. Payment history should recoginze and show any payments , regardless of the status, making it easier to troubleshoot and understand what's happened. (in this case tx is stuck in the mempool) (higher prio) ### How did you encounter this bug? 1. Create a payment request 2. Pay 3. Invoice is invalid 4. Check payment request UI, you'll see pending 5. No payment history is shown ### Relevant log output _No response_ ### What browser do you use? _No response_ ### Additional information ### Are you sure this is a bug report? - [X] I confirm this is a bug report
issue_comments:
So there are two bugs in one issue:
1. Why is it still pending when the invoice is invalid
2. Why the payment doesn't appear.

For `2.` the PR https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/pull/6412 will fix it.

For `1`, a workaround is for the merchant to mark the invoice as Settled, then mark it as Invalid again.
There is an assumption in Payment Request and Crowdfund that an invalid invoice should still be accounted for. But not for `Invalid (marked)`. This is on purpose, and I think we can change this, but if we were deliberate into having this behavior, I feel that it will impact some people.

issue_title: [Bug]: email activation for new users can't be turned of for private registration via API
issue_body:
### What is your BTCPay version?

BTCPay Server v2.0.3+558f45a48

### How did you deploy BTCPay Server?

docker

### What happened?

A [user on Telegram](https://t.me/btcpayserver/81254) reported that he's missing an API call of activating newly created user accounts (with API created).
After some research by @pavlenex and myself we found that the Option "Email confirmation required" can only be disabled if "Enable public user registration" is on in server settings. However there should be no self registration enabled as users are created via the API.

It seems that this use case was overlooked here.

I think it would be better to allow toggle of email confirmation requirement independently if public registration is enabled.

### How did you encounter this bug?

I didn't, the above mentioned telegram user did.

### Relevant log output

```shell
none
```


### What browser do you use?

not specified

### Additional information

none

### Are you sure this is a bug report?

- [X] I confirm this is a bug report
issue_comments:
> I think it would be better to allow toggle of email confirmation requirement independently if public registration is enabled.

I don't think this should be a desired outcome, and it wouldn't fix the underlaying problem. There should be some sort of explanation in the API or the error that this needs to be enabled, since from what I understand ,the user leveraging API had no context on what's happening.

I wasn't aware of this issue created. 
After your explanation on TG that the activation of the user only impacts GUI, this is not an issue for me. 
It took some time to figure this out, so perhaps some additional info in the docs could be of help. 
Or an option to enable the account (via an additional field or automatically) when password is provided.

I did enable public registration before temporary indeed, since I was playing around.
I did run some different scenarios:
```
Public Registration True, Admin Approval False:
Users_ToggleUserApproval:  400 - {"code":"invalid-state","message":"Approving user failed"}

Public Registration True, Admin Approval True:
Users_ToggleUserApproval:  200 - succesfull API call

Public Registration False, Admin Approval False:
Users_ToggleUserApproval:  400 - {"code":"invalid-state","message":"Approving user failed"}

Public Registration False, Admin Approval True:
Users_ToggleUserApproval:  200 - succesfull API call
```

I'm not sure if that is what you expect? 

Next to that in none of the successful approval scenario's, the 'Pending Invitation' user became an 'Active' user.
@lightningcheckout Yes, these are expected to behave this way. I've added #6425 to provide more precise error messages for the approval endpoint to distinguish why approval failed.

issue_title: [Bug]: Changing number of confirms required for one coin changes it for all of them.
issue_body: ### What is your BTCPay version? © BTCPay Server v1.13.5+30d91d0bd (Altcoins) ### How did you deploy BTCPay Server? Azure ### What happened? Hello, I have several coins integrated into BTC Pay Server. When I change the number of confirms required for settlement on one of the coins, it changes that for all of them. So for example if I change LTC to 0, it also changes it to 0 for BTC, DOGE, and Liquid. ### How did you encounter this bug? asdf ### Relevant log output _No response_ ### What browser do you use? _No response_ ### Additional information _No response_ ### Are you sure this is a bug report? - [X] I confirm this is a bug report
issue_comments: This is not a bug.

I wanted to add zero conf on LTC because there is no rbf risk. Then all I had to do was create a script to make sure the fee set with LTC payments was sufficiently high, thus enabling instant payments with LTC.

Additionally, if the current is intended behavior, it is certainly misleading because the setting is configurable in each individual wallet which leads merchants to believe when they set it in one wallet it shouldn't apply to all other wallets.

But okay!

Yeah, you're right, I completely forgot we changed that part of the code. Apologies. 

@dennisreimann this one needs to be investigated, when we moved store confirmations into settings for every payment method, then it needs to reflect changes per payment method, or otherwise be moved to general store settings.

Checked some more, the above goes for all of the payment settings:

issue_title: Problems with invitation and accepting invitation to the server by user
issue_body:
What is your BTCPay version?
BTCPay Server 2.0.0

How did you deploy BTCPay Server?
Docker through dev env

I'm inviting users directly from the store and sending them the generated link on the 2.0 version of BTCPay Server, but I've encountered a few issues:

1. **404 Error on Follow-Up Visits:**  
   If the user clicks the link but doesn’t complete the form immediately, follow-up visits return a 404 error.  
   **Expected behavior:** The page should only become unavailable after the user sets their password. Ideally, there should be a clearer error message like "Invite expired" rather than a generic 404.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a520a7da-b4c1-495d-9e8e-f2e51d82edd3

2. **Re-inviting Deleted Users:**  
   When inviting users from the Store page, if you delete them and then try to re-invite, it’s not obvious why the invitation link isn’t working. Additionally, if you only have access to Store Users and not Server Users, you won’t be able to remove the user you added, which complicates managing users.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/92949242-73fb-4197-a899-57e90783b40e

3. **Incorrect Invitation URL for Localhost:**  
   When visiting the user page while an invitation is pending, the short Invitation URL appears incorrect for localhost. 

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f08b9561-d49b-4d61-a109-357bfefbe5a2
issue_comments: Thanks for the detailed report. I'll take care.

@rockstardev I've added the fixes to my current invitation flow PR #6233, can you give this a try?

> 404 Error on Follow-Up Visits

I've made it so that the invitation token is now only cancelled once the user can sign in. This means after confirming the email and setting a password. The link will work until then, which is the expoected behaviour I'd say.

> Re-inviting Deleted Users

I've added a note to the success message, stating that the user is already registered on the server and hence no invite email has been sent. The situation about only being able to work with users on the store level is a bit tricky, because I wouldn't go as far as deleting users once they do not belong to any store anymore. 

 > Incorrect Invitation URL for Localhost
 
Already fixed by the previous commits of the PR :)

Great job, I've tested the functionality it works well. 

The only minor issue I've noticed is that if you navigate to the page and then refresh it, you won’t see the `Invitation accepted. Please set your password.` message, since the `UIAccountController` sets the notice.

This isn’t a big deal for now, but I’d love to do a more detailed review with you on the flow of invitations, as I'm a bit confused by the switches in code (especially the new `FinalizeInvitationIfApplicable` method).

I've also tested adding a user multiple times, and while it's useful to display `The user is already registered on this server, so they didn't receive an invitation email` when adding new users in the store, it can be confusing for old flows - when you know user is already on the server and you just want to add them to the store.

For that reason, I suggest reverting the `Add hint about already registered users` commit, and we can address #2 in a different way down the line.

**TL;DR, here’s what I recommend:**
- Revert the `Add hint about already registered users` commit.
- Proceed with merging the PR based on the feedback, as the main issue with the invite link not working has been resolved.
- Close this issue once you've read this response
I've dropped the additional message commit as discussed here. Also added a note to the pull request, that it closes this issue when merged.

issue_title: [Bug]: Shopify Integration: BTCPay invoice not generated until user clicks the button
issue_body:
### What is your BTCPay version?

This issue is written on behalf of a Voltage user from uncute.com having issues with the shopify checkout.

BTCPay version 1.13.3
Running BTCPayServer from Voltage service.

The User says: 
BTCpay invoice is not created until users clicks on the button injected by the js in the order status page.
- Desirable solution: Triggering the creation of the invoice as soon as the order is placed using Webhook. This way it's not dependent on end users's UI.
- Easy solution: in the script added to order status page, call function getOrCheckInvoice when the scripts load (get request to trigger invoice creation).

My note:
This is important for a secondary reason because the expiry timer needs kicked off immediately, not when button is clicked. By updating this behavior, inventory management will also be more accurate. 

Worked with @pavlenex and customer on July 12, 2024 conference call.
issue_comments:
Feedback from the user:
 Main issue: BTCpay invoice is not created until users clicks on the button injected by the js in the order status page.
- Desirable solution: Triggering the creation of the invoice as soon as the order is placed using Webhook. This way it's not dependent on end users's UI.
- Easy solution: in the script added to order status page, call function getOrCheckInvoice when the scripts load (get request to trigger invoice creation).

issue_body:
BTCPay version 1.13.3
Running BTCPayServer from Voltage service. 

When a Shopify cancel timer hits zero, the button to generate an invoice still allows an invoice to be generated. 

1. Cancelled Shopify Order
2. Clicked PayWith BTCPay button on the Shopify checkout page. The invoice was generated even though the order was cancelled. 
 

This issue is written on behalf of a Voltage user from uncute.com having issues with the shopify checkout.

issue_title: Default printing template for Keypad now produces extra whitespace on Bitcoinize POS
issue_body:
During our conference representation in Hong Kong, we noticed that extra lines in receipts are causing the Bitcoinize POS to print receipts with additional whitespace.

![image](https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/assets/5191402/4084cc1d-7602-4163-bbd4-bd24ecfa93b2)

To address this issue, I've created a commit that removes duplicate lines (Item, Subtotal) when unnecessary to ensure that receipts are printed without the extra space - https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/commit/ef4f7b8c19da65d4b18aa6f5f45e59791d3cea19

![image](https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/assets/5191402/8c77f324-a00f-463d-bdfd-b36b89f1856e)

We need to figure out how to address this for 2.0 - since default configuration right now would cause everyone deploying that version to have issues with extra receipt paper usage.

issue_title: [Bug][UI]: Invoices could be permanently filtered via SearchTerm stored in cookies
issue_body:
### What is your BTCPay version?

I'm running BTCPay Server v1.13.1+ca55e1f30

### How did you deploy BTCPay Server?

I'm running BTCPay Server on dedicated VPS deployed with Docker

### What happened?

If you are filtering out Invoices list by clicking "Invoices" in Requests list - it goes into cookies and Invoices list became filtered forever

### How did you encounter this bug?

1. Install BTCPay server
2. Create store and wallet
3. Make some invoices
4. Make a Payment Request
5. Open that request and try to pay it, that should create invoices with Order Id linked to request - e.g. PAY_REQUEST_ID
6. Observe that now we have different invoices in Invoices list
7. Go to Requests page, click three dots at the end of the request row and click "Invoices"
8. Observe Invoices list which only contain invoices that linked to the request
9. Click anywhere else on the web UI and click again on the Invoices - you should return to the Invoices list which will be still filtered by the request with no obvious way to reset this filter

### Relevant log output

_No response_

### What browser do you use?

Chrome 124.0.6367.119

### Additional information

There are three ways to reset filter:
1. LogOut-LogIn
2. Put focus on search input in Invoices page, press Enter, that will bring params into URL, delete SearchTerm content in URL, press Enter to reload page
3. Delete SearchTerm field from cookies

### Are you sure this is a bug report?

- [X] I confirm this is a bug report

issue_title: [Bug]: UX: Possible double sent on pull-payments/payouts
issue_body:
### What is your BTCPay version?

v1.13.0

### How did you deploy BTCPay Server?

docker

### What happened?

When doing pull-payments and having a payout processor enabled it could lead to double sending the transaction as after clicking button "approve + pay" the user is redirected to sign transaction screen where it would sign it a scecond time, the processor already sent the tx on click of the button in the previous step.

Problem description from Nicolas:

> The problem is: The automated payout processor will automatically pay from a hotwallet any payout that become approved. 
> 
> When the user click on the "Approve & Pay", he approves the payout, and thus the automated payout processor send the money. Then the user not noticing it, proceed with doing his own transaction.

### How did you encounter this bug?

1. have payout processor enabled
2. create a pull payment (add payout address)
3. in payouts: approve + pay the pull (sends first tx)
4. on sign tx screen, sign and broadcast (sends second tx)

### Relevant log output

_No response_

### What browser do you use?

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

### Are you sure this is a bug report?

- [X] I confirm this is a bug report
issue_comments: UX wise, would it make sense to not proceed to signing screen when payout processor is enabled or just not show it at all? @dennisreimann and @dstrukt likely have better insights how to handle it.
Yup, agreed. We should alter that flow and instead of sending the user to the signing proces, it should notify them about the payment getting handled via the automated payout processor.

issue_title: [Bug]: email not obfuscated when adding "contact us" information
issue_body:
### What is your BTCPay version?

v1.13.0-rc2

### How did you deploy BTCPay Server?

docker

### What happened?

When adding a contact mail in server settings -> branding and it is displayed on the login page the email is not obfuscated and so it can be scraped by webcrawlers and get added to spam lists.

### How did you encounter this bug?

1. add email to server settings -> branding
2. on login page check the "contact us" link

_No response_

### What browser do you use?

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

- [X] I confirm this is a bug report
issue_comments: What can or should be done about this?

The basic idea to prevent this is to output obfuscated garbage on initial page load and html source and then make it a proper mailto link with javascript. Maybe there is something prebuilt for vue.js, the basic idea is like this https://www.npmjs.com/package/email-scramble

Obviously this won't work if the crawler parses JS like a webbrowser. Safest thing there would be a contact form but there you then have again that spam problem where you need some kind of captcha or trap to prevent spam :disappointed:
Maybe we should then simply remove it from the public pages and only handle it via #5866? @pavlenex
If the user forgets his password and smtp is not setup it might be the only way to contact the owner though? :sweat_smile:

issue_title: Specify `mailto:` prefix for emails in `Server Settings > Branding > Contact`
issue_body: We should specify that for emails `mailto:` needs to be added, otherwise if user adds email@email.com and someone clicks, `contact us` on login page, it gives 404. Even better if we can recongize it's an email and no need for mailto. **To replicate:** 1. In Server Settings > Branding > Contact us add email@test.com 2. Log out 3. Go to login page, click contact us 4. 404 5. To fix add mailto:email.test.com ##Solution: - Recognize it's email and apply mailto so user doesn't have to, - less ideal: add text that for emails mailto: prefix is required. Feature Introduced in this PR https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/pull/5718
issue_comments: @TChukwuleta Here's another good first issue if you'd like to tackle it. It's time sensitive since we tend to release in the next 10 days or so.
Sure @pavlenex

issue_title: [Bug]: Connecting a Trezor One doesn't prompt for entering a passphrase
issue_body:
### What is your BTCPay version?

v1.12.5+a86165ac0

### How did you deploy BTCPay Server?

docker via the setup script: https://docs.btcpayserver.org/Docker/ 

### What happened?

You cannot choose one of your passphrase-based wallets in Trezor One when trying to connect the hardware wallet via the "Connect hardware wallet" option when setting up the server. IIRC these are BIP39 passphrase wallets, and is a common concept used both as a security measure and as a convenience to separate your wallets via a single hardware device.

There's an "Account" slider where I can input  0,1,2 etc., but it seems unrelated to this issue.

### How did you encounter this bug?

1. I installed the server
2. Registered with user/pass
3. Tried to connect my wallet

### Relevant log output

_No response_

### What browser do you use?

Chrome 122.0.6261.70 (Official Build) (64-bit)

### Additional information

device shows as "trezor_1" on the connection page, once Vault detects it.

@pavlenex suggested I open this as a bug report, most probably the feature already exists but it isn't enabled somehow?

This issue corrects #5793's title



### Are you sure this is a bug report?

- [X] I confirm this is a bug report
issue_comments:
~So the issue is that before Trezor could require passphrase, we would detect this and show the prompt.~
~Now however, the passphrase is optional and doesn't require it, so we never ask for it.~

On Trezor T, the passphrase is asked. On Trezor One, it isn't. We should always ask for it then.
will be in next minor release

issue_title: [Bug]: "Onion Available" redirect in Tor Browser does not work for stores with custom subdomain
issue_body:
### What is your BTCPay version?

I'm running BTCPay Server v1.12.5+a86165ac0 

### How did you deploy BTCPay Server?

Lunanode

### What happened?

I love that BTCPay supports Tor and is available over onion by default. When navigating to a BTCPay store in the Tor Browser, I see the "Onion available" button. When clicking on it, I expect to be redirected to the onion page of the store. This works well unless the store I'm navigating is configured on a subdomain that is not the store's primary domain.

### How did you encounter this bug?

1. Go to [https://btcpay.containerbrewing.ca/](https://btcpay.containerbrewing.ca/) in the Tor Browser
2. Click on "Onion available" in the address bar
EXPECTED: 3. See the same store, but with an onion address
ACTUAL: 3. Get redirected to BTCPay's login page

### Relevant log output

_No response_

### What browser do you use?

Tor Browser `13.0.9 (based on Mozilla Firefox 115.7.0esr)`

### Additional information

_No response_

### Are you sure this is a bug report?

- [X] I confirm this is a bug report
issue_comments: I think this is expected behaviour, afaik we can't make it work due to limitations of how onion addresses work, @Kukks can expand on technical details, but pretty sure this came up once or twice already and we couldn't resolve it, hence I'll close it.
I think the error comes from the redirect, if setting a custom domain for a point of sale allowed that PoS to be available _in addition_ to its "native" url, the onion shouldn't be affected either.
@dennisreimann @NicolasDorier any thoughts?

I think this might be actually two problems: For one, the hidden service of the instance does not seem to work — at least I cannot access `http://vc6ggjiyy33hq5wgwosw3d6x265tsdpxgqbn6b4wigcv5otrexj44qqd.onion/login`, which should work regardless of the POS app redirects.

The second problem here is connected to the redirects: We must exclude Tor requests from the canonical domain redirects [here](https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/blob/master/BTCPayServer/Filters/DomainMappingConstraintAttribute.cs#L39). I'll send a PR to fix this issue.

issue_title: [Bug]: Unable to place "mailto" hyperlink into store description
issue_body: ### What is your BTCPay version? I'm running BTCPay Server v1.12.5+a86165ac0 ### How did you deploy BTCPay Server? Lunanode ### What happened? I'm editing the description of a web store. I am using the editor to insert a hyperlink `mailto:email@email.com`. I save the edits and view the store, but I can't click on or see the hyperlink I created. In the source, I can see: `please send an \u003ca href=\u0022mailto:email@email.com\u0022\u003eemail\u003c/a\u003e by clicking here` I placed a hyperlink to `https://btcpay.containerbrewing.ca` somewhere else in the text, and it shows up properly. issue_title: When switching from new checkout to classic, use a custom css and go back to new checkout, the new checkout renders legacy css instead of branding
issue_body:
This is a low-priority issue that I encountered when trying to play around with #5615, but basically if user had branding options with new checkout, then in an unlikely scenario goes back to classic checkout, adds custom css, tries it out, doesn't like it and, switches to new checkout again, the new checkout is a mixture of that legacy checkout css and new branding.

Video shows this better.

It seems that somehow custom css embedded in classic still influences the css in new checkout, which shouldn't be the case as they're distinct. 

https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/assets/36959754/5f8e591f-d8a2-488d-9a3d-a369864b4f6e

issue_title: Classic checkout - custom style doesn't apply to the checkout page
issue_body:
Context:
In BTCPay Server we have two types of checkouts: classic and new one. Classic (Legacy) is kept for backward compatibility with older checkout pages, as some users are using custom css and we want to avoid breaking changes for them.

Problem:
The classic page doesn't render the custom css anymore, not even default themes dark or legacy.

To replicate:
1. In Checkout Appearance, select classic
2. Select one of the pre-defined themes (for example dark)
3. Create an invoice
4. Visit the checkout paeg
5. You'll see that custom styling doesn't' apply.
6. BTCPay Server v1.12.3+f23078df1

```


### What browser do you use?

Firefox on Windows 11

### Additional information

none

### Are you sure this is a bug report?

- [X] I confirm this is a bug report
issue_comments: Replicated this one in 1.12.1 this is quite concerning and should be addressed in the next bug-fix release. Perhaps meanwhile here we can tell people how to uninstall plugins via command line cc @dennisreimann ?

To delete a plugin via CLI one can proceed like this:

```bash
# Go to plugins directory
cd /var/lib/docker/volumes/generated_btcpay_pluginsdir/_data

# List all installed plugins
ls -lA 

# Uninstall a plugin (this only removed it from the file system, it keeps the data in the database)
rm BTCPayServer.Plugins.PluginName
```
Thanks @dennisreimann, I'll make sure to mention that to people.
did emergency release on this 1.12.2

issue_title: Add Payment request link trigger for email rules setting and remove redundant message in the UI
issue_body:
Right now, it's not possible to send a payment request link through an email, even though our UI says it is.
We need to add payment request functionality within the email configuration so that users could send payment links to their clients, it's pretty common feature in invoicing. 


![Screenshot 2023-09-27 at 14 31 08](https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/assets/36959754/1b88f7c4-45f1-4a6e-aacc-8743f9e8ca09)

Furthermore, the message in the payment request UI is redundant.

```The recipient's email. This will send notification mails to the recipient, as configured by the email rules, and include the email in the invoice export data..```


I don't think the other part matters, it's normal that if email is used it should be added to the invoice.

```[tasklist]
### Tasks
- [x] Add payment request setting in email configuration
- [x] Remove the second part of the message The recipient's email. This will send notification mails to the recipient, as configured by the email rules.
```

issue_title: [Bug]: Email rules, Recipients required even if "send mail to buyer"?
issue_body:
### What is your BTCPay version?

BTCPay 1.11.5-rc1

### How did you deploy BTCPay Server?

docker

### What happened?

On the Email Rules page you have the option "Send the email to the buyer, if email was provided to the invoice" 

I wonder if we should make the above "Recipients" field non-required if the option is checked (and do not send any mail if there is no buyer email available) otherwise you will always send a copy to yourself which could be kinda anoying with lots of invoices.

![image](https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/assets/1136761/0734a527-8e6f-4060-9068-aeed1fa0dcaa)

issue_title: [Bug]: Minimal permissions not working as intended
issue_body:
### What is your BTCPay version?

BTCPay 1.11.5-rc1 but also true for previous verison 1.11.4 and likely earlier

### How did you deploy BTCPay Server?

docker

### What happened?

**1.) no "view invoices" permission causes no store access at all**
if that permission is not set then the store won't be accessible at all, also if you have the "manage pull payments" permission

**2.) Sub-permissions won't work:**
E.g. if you have a serverwide role of "pull payment manager" that only has the permission of "create non-approved pull payments" and "archive pull payments" then the "Pull payments" menu entry won't show up at all. But it should?

**3.) Reporting menu item leads to 403**
When you have the "view invoices" permission the "Reporting" menu items shows up but leads to a 403 permission denied page

### How did you encounter this bug?

see above description

_No response_

_No response_

_No response_

issue_comments:
@Kukks sorry for the delay testing this. 

I added a role pull payment manager with permissions: createnonapprovedpp... and archivepp:
- correct sidemenu entry shows up
- correct permission to create an archive pp
- error?: on store dropdown user sees "create store" link and also store creation works
- error?: there is no way for the user to logout as the whole "accound" menu is missing
> error?: on store dropdown user sees "create store" link and also store creation works

As discussed in the dev call this is related to people being able to create stores for themselves. We need to tackle this in a separate PR and also with a policy which can be defined for creating stores.

 > error?: there is no way for the user to logout as the whole "accound" menu is missing
 
I cannot reproduce this on master. For me the account menu shows up and the code also doesn't contain any constraints regarding roles/permissions for the account menu.

Store creation
Yes I think we should do that, e.g. maybe even go so far that admins can configure how many stores can be created which would be helpful for 3rd party hosts. Maybe along with the super admin view for 1.13 we could do that too.

Account
I just tried to reproduce but couldn't, the Account link is here. Seems I made had some hallucinations. All good. 

So, case closed for now, will do more testing with permissions.

issue_title: [Bug]: Payment request doesn't have a concept of `processing` status, goes immediately to settled for on-chain payments
issue_body:
### What is your BTCPay version?

 BTCPay Server v1.11.2+1c5fcfe09

### How did you deploy BTCPay Server?

Docker

### What happened?

When a payment request is paid on-chain, the status doesn't get to `processing` instead goes to `settled`.

Two problems:

issue_body:
### What is your BTCPay version?

BTCPay Server 1.11.1 + 1.10.3

docker

### Relevant log output

_No response_

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

### Are you sure this is a bug report?

- [X] I confirm this is a bug report
issue_comments: @petzsch can you try to replicate this?

Can confirm: I'm also unable to remove a label once I've added it to a tx.

Something like a small X when hovering over a label would be helpful.

Maybe it's too simple to even notice, because you can just use the backspace/delete key. You can also select  and then remove it.

![g](https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/assets/886/35c7aeec-8c63-4031-94e9-d3b33a3f3001)

Haha, works that way :green_heart: 

I saw the "selected mode" after clicking on the label but was too dumb to try backspace or del keys. Question is if users and some of us can't figure this out should we maybe make it remove on click instead of highlighting? This only works as long is there is no other feature planned e.g. by clicking on the label it will auto filter only that label etc. so maybe showing an x on hover and making it clickable (like @petzsch suggested)? 

Or maybe we just wait if this issue pops up again and decide to refactor then.

current UI for deleting labels seems a bit unintiuitiv. I also think we should consider changing it.

The little x which shows on hover which I originally proposed, might not work for mobile users.
personal opinion: Delete on click sounds about right to me.

Or is there any other functionality (planned) with selected labels that we needed the selected state for?

issue_title: [Bug]: Reporting: rounding and output issue
issue_body:
### What is your BTCPay version?

BTCPay 1.11.0-rc4

### How did you deploy BTCPay Server?

docker

### What happened?

**1. Rounding issue:**
total of lightning address seems to have a rounding isssue 
![image](https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/assets/1136761/0b89377f-98de-47d0-88bc-ed8419cc7dba)

**2. Formatting issue**
Here the 0,00000001 are output as 1e-8 which also ends up in the CSV export. Imo we should enforce decimals here so it is consistent and not make the consumer of the export handle formatting of the values? 
![image](https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/assets/1136761/74c2552b-3bad-4ad1-87ec-e56ee823b855)

**3. Inconsistency / clarification on "products sold" export:**
![image](https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/assets/1136761/fbedffdd-7fc2-4563-974c-c1538087600a)

1)  products sold:
"sold" a bit misleading as it shows unconfirmed invoices with status new? (or maybe do not show state new at all here?)

2) state + quantity:
state "new" is ok if it should be shown at all, in this case but the quantity is not, there are 3 rooibos sells open 

3) raw data:
also misses the other new invoices, seems to only count it once per product?

To discuss/decide:
question is if new invoices should be shown at all;

also if e.g. 2 rooibos invoices expire and others are not the state "expired" is not shown at all, only state "new"; unsure if all states should be there or only a few? 

**4. CSV output thoughts (maybe non-issues)**
Currently the CSV output does not put strings into quotes and uses `,` as a separator. 
```
Date,InvoiceId,OrderId,PaymentType,PaymentId,Confirmed,Address,Crypto,CryptoAmount,NetworkFee,LightningAddress,Currency,CurrencyAmount,Rate
2023-03-31 12:15:51,STyQudaVQJDvzGepwmcMGs,729,On-Chain,72a2dfc9b5418663b2bfea1bc48aa24173948aead47bcd2c7821a89143a007d6-1,true,mwX1BypzYcFtfNMy3X1oquTtP7HPQWrjZh,BTC,0.00036571,0,,USD,10.19,27863>
2023-03-31 01:55:36,7J7hGs29id2BaY5TdBC5B4,731,On-Chain,04cc6904ed14c78a1b7418ac68fe633477bcb08a0d68c7eadfbdd073c7818bee-0,true,mks8bxfePzaUD8xAXYFzwBRewy8H4iVeVn,BTC,0.00071459,0,,USD,19.98,27960>
2023-06-27 08:54:24,JPqugmiUdQPtor7g4KLQjk,,Lightning,4b4953cc3ece9a6d0d6b64798d8bc7bbf3f35268a484fc3bca9ff00900a98239,true,,BTC,1e-8,0,test@testnet.demo.btcpay.tech,USD,0.00031,30718
2023-06-27 08:54:28,Mca5izZambMnamQBwKrDHT,,Lightning,5340bb680f7de19e25ddd373f014b33afcc5e7c29c03d5258ea36fe86f1bfbf8,true,,BTC,0.00196674,0,test@testnet.demo.btcpay.tech,USD,60.41,30718

```

First, no `"` for strings could cause issues if your product name on Products sold output has `,` in it as it would be interpreted as sparator if there is no automatic escaping happening on the export.

Second, using `,` as separator maybe problematic if big fiat values such as 10,000.30 could cause problems. This is a non issue if the export escapes the output or does no amercan style amout formatting at all. But maybe less error prone to use a `;` as separator? 


### How did you encounter this bug?

In new reporting feature

### Relevant log output

_No response_

### What browser do you use?

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

### Are you sure this is a bug report?

- [X] I confirm this is a bug report
issue_comments:
The rounding issue for reports is expected. The math is using Javascript, which has trouble with rounding.

Since it's not easy to fix in JS, I believe I will move the math on server side in next major.
I moved this to 1.12.0, because this isn't easy to fix for a minor.

issue_title: [Dashboard] Support for Custom Ordering of Dashboard widgets

issue_title: [Bug]: Do not throw error 500 if the transaction list of wallet is timing out
issue_body:
### What is your BTCPay version?

1.9.3

### How did you deploy BTCPay Server?

docker

### What happened?

A big wallet timeout on the wallet page.

### How did you encounter this bug?

Have a big wallet, then go to the transaction list of the wallet, see an error 500

We should have a proper fallback in this situation. There are some pattern of wallet that may timeout like this, it is caused because we do not paginate based on the date.

Another idea would just to ajaxify the page. @dennisreimann do you think we can put that in next release?

### Relevant log output

```shell
fail: Microsoft.AspNetCore.Server.Kestrel: Connection id "0HMQG63U0LBDI", Request id "0HMQG63U0LBDI:00000002": An unhandled exception was thrown by the application.
Npgsql.NpgsqlException (0x80004005): Exception while reading from stream
 ---> System.TimeoutException: Timeout during reading attempt
   at Npgsql.Internal.NpgsqlConnector.<ReadMessage>g__ReadMessageLong|215_0(NpgsqlConnector connector, Boolean async, DataRowLoadingMode dataRowLoadingMode, Boolean readingNotifications, Boolean isReadingPrependedMessage)
   at Npgsql.NpgsqlDataReader.<ReadMessage>g__ReadMessageSequential|44_0(NpgsqlConnector connector, Boolean async)
   at Npgsql.NpgsqlDataReader.NextResult(Boolean async, Boolean isConsuming, CancellationToken cancellationToken)
   at Npgsql.NpgsqlCommand.ExecuteReader(CommandBehavior behavior, Boolean async, CancellationToken cancellationToken)
   at Npgsql.NpgsqlCommand.ExecuteReader(CommandBehavior behavior, Boolean async, CancellationToken cancellationToken)
   at Npgsql.NpgsqlCommand.ExecuteDbDataReaderAsync(CommandBehavior behavior, CancellationToken cancellationToken)
   at Dapper.SqlMapper.QueryAsync[T](IDbConnection cnn, Type effectiveType, CommandDefinition command) in /_/Dapper/SqlMapper.Async.cs:line 418
   at BTCPayServer.Services.Wallets.BTCPayWallet.FetchTransactionHistory(DerivationStrategyBase derivationStrategyBase, Nullable`1 skip, Nullable`1 count, Nullable`1 interval) in /source/BTCPayServer/Services/Wallets/BTCPayWallet.cs:line 246
```

### Workaround

1. Sweep your wallet by browsing directly to `https://yourserver/wallets/<walletid>/send`
2. Create a new emtpy wallet by browsing `https://yourserver/stores/<storeid>/onchain/BTC/settings`

Luna

### What happened?

Swapping between Invoices and Dashboard view made me aware of the inconsistency. 

### How did you encounter this bug?

Log in to your BTCPay Server, open Dashboard. 
Here you'll find " Recent Invoices " and their current states. 
There's a label to it when settled or expired. 

However when you open the Invoice view, these labels when it is a LN invoice get a little Lightning Emoji next to it, clear visual indicator it was done through lightning. 

Looking back at the dashboard view, these Lightning bolts are missing on the Label settled. 
This might be performance issue if added, but for now, to me looks like inconsistency issue. 

There's no urge for this apart from a consistent way of displaying labels.

issue_title: If the store default payment method is set and invoice is created without setting it, null is returned as default payment method.
issue_body:
### Discussed in https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/discussions/4946
Originally posted by **learntheropes** May 4, 2023 If I create an invoice without providing the `defaultPaymentMethod` property and I have a `"defaultPaymentMethod": "BTC-LightningNetwork"` in my store, I get `null` as default payment method. Is this the expected behavior? Should I open a feature request to have `BTC-LightningNetwork` returned, avoiding making an extra API call to get this info? Or should I open a bug report because the documentation says: `Default payment method set for the store is used if this parameter is not specified.`. How it is used if the returned value is `null`? Replication: 1. Check the store setting: ```bash curl --location --request GET 'https://testnet.demo.btcpayserver.org/api/v1/stores/4hEu1jrrC9VKLpLjcVtdLYf1FZTb3bqgUoERboCnGbXE' \ --header 'Authorization: token ' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' ``` Response body, where we see that the default payment method is `BTC-LightningNetwork`: ```json { "id": "4hEu1jrrC9VKLpLjcVtdLYf1FZTb3bqgUoERboCnGbXE", "name": "booking learntheropes testnet", "website": "https://booking.learntheropes.xyz/", "invoiceExpiration": 900, "displayExpirationTimer": 300, "monitoringExpiration": 86400, "speedPolicy": "MediumSpeed", "lightningDescriptionTemplate": "Paid to {StoreName} (Order ID: {OrderId})", "paymentTolerance": 0.0, "anyoneCanCreateInvoice": false, "defaultCurrency": null, "requiresRefundEmail": false, "checkoutType": "V2", "lightningAmountInSatoshi": false, "lightningPrivateRouteHints": false, "onChainWithLnInvoiceFallback": false, "lazyPaymentMethods": false, "redirectAutomatically": false, "showRecommendedFee": true, "recommendedFeeBlockTarget": 1, "defaultPaymentMethod": "BTC-LightningNetwork", "defaultLang": "en", "customLogo": null, "customCSS": null, "htmlTitle": null, "networkFeeMode": "MultiplePaymentsOnly", "paymentMethodCriteria": [ { "paymentMethod": "BTC", "currencyCode": "USD", "amount": "0", "above": true }, { "paymentMethod": "BTC-LightningNetwork", "currencyCode": "USD", "amount": "0", "above": true }, { "paymentMethod": "BTC-LNURLPAY", "currencyCode": "USD", "amount": "0", "above": true } ], "payJoinEnabled": false, "receipt": { "enabled": true, "showQR": true, "showPayments": true } } ``` 2. Create an invoice without settings the default payment method ```bash curl --location --request POST 'https://testnet.demo.btcpayserver.org/api/v1/stores/4hEu1jrrC9VKLpLjcVtdLYf1FZTb3bqgUoERboCnGbXE/invoices' \ --header 'Authorization: token ' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data-raw '{ "currency": "USD", "amount": 100, "metadata": { "buyerEmail": "foo@bar.com" }, "checkout": { "redirectAutomatically": false, "requiresRefundEmail": true } }' ``` Get a body with `null` as default payment method. ```json { "id": "NQE1yURYGLHXo1gJ8HDTGs", "storeId": "4hEu1jrrC9VKLpLjcVtdLYf1FZTb3bqgUoERboCnGbXE", "amount": "100", "checkoutLink": "https://testnet.demo.btcpayserver.org/i/NQE1yURYGLHXo1gJ8HDTGs", "status": "New", "additionalStatus": "None", "monitoringExpiration": 1683285026, "expirationTime": 1683198626, "createdTime": 1683197726, "availableStatusesForManualMarking": [ "Settled", "Invalid" ], "archived": false, "type": "Standard", "currency": "USD", "metadata": { "buyerEmail": "foo@bar.com" }, "checkout": { "speedPolicy": "MediumSpeed", "paymentMethods": [ "BTC", "BTC-LightningNetwork" ], "defaultPaymentMethod": null, "expirationMinutes": 15, "monitoringMinutes": 1440, "paymentTolerance": 0.0, "redirectURL": null, "redirectAutomatically": false, "requiresRefundEmail": true, "defaultLanguage": null, "checkoutType": null }, "receipt": { "enabled": null, "showQR": null, "showPayments": null } } ```
issue_comments: ACK, but just made a small note: https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/discussions/4946#discussioncomment-5832943

issue_title: [Bug]: Clicking on "Return to {App Name}" returns "No Connection with that ID"
issue_body:
### What is your BTCPay version?

BTCPay Server v1.9.2+6193835ea 

### How did you deploy BTCPay Server?

On Lunanode using the Lunanode installer

### What happened?

I have a crowdfund set up [for example this one](https://we.encrypt.cash/apps/1Chngwii4XMnueujJpWk78fMe75/crowdfund).

I can successfully generate invoices and pay them, but upon clicking on "Return to {App Name}", I'm directed to a page with the URL `https://we.encrypt.cash/apps/hub?id=av_oczbja99i2mXl2iuDlw`, which displays the error `No Connection with that ID`.

### How did you encounter this bug?

1. Generate invoice
2. Pay invoice
3. Click on "Return to {App Name}

### Relevant log output

issue_comments:
Hey thanks for filing bug! 

Did you perhaps set the root domain of your BTCPayServer to this specific crowdfund app? 
I've noticed similar behaviour in this set up :)
This looks like a case in which the return URL doesn't get generated correctly and the web socket endpoint is used instead of the app URL.
Hi! Thank you for taking a look. The crowdfund app is not running on the root domain or on an external domain. The URL is like this: https://we.encrypt.cash/apps/1Chngwii4XMnueujJpWk78fMe75/crowdfund
I can confirm that this is easily reproducible without the domain mapping feature. More info in the fix PR.

issue_title: [Bug]: New receipt in Shop + cart doesnt display items properly and missing additional info.
issue_body:
### What is your BTCPay version?

1.9.1

### How did you deploy BTCPay Server?

LunaNode standard deployment 

### What happened?

Using the PoS app in the Shop + Cart configuration the following happens with receipts. 
When there's only 1 item purchased, the name of the item does not get displayed, but rather the title of Cart and item price.





### How did you encounter this bug?

Whenever selecting just 1 item, no discounts or tips, the receipt shows ;
![1](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/102448109/232549157-8cb0cc68-5c02-4153-802f-1f6e539e9b66.jpg)

When you add discount or tips, the selected items name doesn't show ; 
![2](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/102448109/232549437-a640f310-5550-475a-9671-d77f7318cde4.png)

However when you select multiple items, it looks as follows ; 
![3](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/102448109/232549353-25dfdd9c-0b11-426c-8913-c5b4c2f7f11e.jpg)

Additionally, the custom form data doesn't get represented on the receipt


### Relevant log output

_No response_

### What browser do you use?

firefox. 

### Additional information

Additionally to the Shop + cart config. If you use the product list only, the receipt looks as follows : 
![4](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/102448109/232550574-02f862e3-2672-470a-924e-3d15c8286d09.jpg)


### Are you sure this is a bug report?

- [X] I confirm this is a bug report
issue_comments:
> Additionally, the custom form data doesn't get represented on the receipt

Hello, the custom form data is not displayed on the receipt in version 1.9.2 although the cart details from the POS store are now displayed correctly. How can the custom form data be included on the receipt?

> > Additionally, the custom form data doesn't get represented on the receipt
> 
> Hello, the custom form data is not displayed on the receipt in version 1.9.2 although the cart details from the POS store are now displayed correctly. How can the custom form data be included on the receipt?

Custom form data is not meant to be displayed on the receipt by default. However you can mirror entries from the form to the receipt by following the example at https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/pull/4774#issue-1625306770

> > > Additionally, the custom form data doesn't get represented on the receipt
> > 
> > 
> > Hello, the custom form data is not displayed on the receipt in version 1.9.2 although the cart details from the POS store are now displayed correctly. How can the custom form data be included on the receipt?
> 
> Custom form data is not meant to be displayed on the receipt by default. However you can mirror entries from the form to the receipt by following the example at [#4774 (comment)](https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/pull/4774#issue-1625306770)

I tried mirroring more than the email address, but it threw an error: "Form config was invalid: Form contains duplicate field names 'receiptData_buyerEmail')"

If I want more than buyer email to appear on the receipt page, how should I modify your json code? The code that threw an error is here: https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/pull/4774#issuecomment-1532270898 Do I need to include "name": "receiptData" for every field? I used it once and included all the fields below. It would be super helpful if you could show an example with two fields being mirrored, to understand how to modify it.

issue_title: [Bug]: standard Tea shop (PoS app) first item breaks after going from shop + cart to just shop
issue_body:

BTCPay Server v1.9.0 and BTCPay Server v1.9.1

### How did you deploy BTCPay Server?

Luna node standard deployment

### What happened?

After trying to figure out the new receipt situation, i switched between shop + cart to just a shop without cart.

Since this swap, the first item in the shop list brings a 404 page. 

### How did you encounter this bug?

Create a PoS shop. 

Switch to a Product list + cart. 
Save
Buy product 1 in the list ( or at least mark as settled on that invoice) 
Switch to just Product list. 
Save

it seems however, it breaks only in the same view i had open before. 
If you reopen the view from the PoS it seems fine?

### Relevant log output

_No response_

### What browser do you use?

_No response_

### Additional information

petzsch and i were going through some motions of the shop and encountered this bug. 
Petzsch runs 1.9.1 and i'm running on the 1.9.0 version. 

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/102448109/232529657-ad9e8663-6bff-43d7-b076-78e6cb5e3507.png)


### Are you sure this is a bug report?

- [X] I confirm this is a bug report
issue_comments:
Problem does not occur when Hard-Reloading (Ctrl - Shift - R in Firefox) the page after changing from product list with cart to product list.

Also doesn't occur when closing the shop tabs and re-opening them through the "View" button from the backend.

So it could be a caching issue. Perhaps something with LocalStorage? 404 (NotFound) seems to be the kind of [standard reply](https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/blob/master/BTCPayServer/Plugins/PointOfSale/Controllers/UIPointOfSaleController.cs#L198) if something went wrong on this PoS.

**UPDATE:** What also stands out is that only the first product is affected. In the GUI these differences between the first and second article stood out: (look at RequiresRefundEMail in the first and second occurance)

![grafik](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1374810/232541079-1f73305b-2db6-4715-a156-cc04bea946cf.png)

EDIT: This doesn't look right. There is no RequiresRefundEMail to be inherited from the Store as it was scraped, right?
https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/blob/43436fc49e4aff72a2af9da0f002b85df3ce5270/BTCPayServer/Views/Shared/PointOfSale/Public/Static.cshtml#L107
and

https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/blob/master/BTCPayServer/Plugins/PointOfSale/Models/ViewPointOfSaleViewModel.cs#L70

I can't reproduce this.

1. Create a new PoS with cart view
2. Buy an item, see the receipt, go back to PoS view
3. Change the PoS to product via
4. F5 on the PoS view page

Then the first item works. That said, the HTML @petzsch  show isn't right, but I can't reproduce it.

I tried the other way around, where I create a pos without cart, pay and then switch on the pos with cart.
It works.
I cannot reproduce this either.

For me it wasn't reproducable on Chrome either.
Just on Firefox.

issue_title: [Bug]: "BTC: Payment method unavailable (Impossible to create lightning invoice (Object reference not set to an instance of an object.))"
issue_body:
### What is your BTCPay version?

1.7.12

### How did you deploy BTCPay Server?

Manual

### What happened?

Switched Lightning back-end to LNDHub, cannot create Lightning invoices anymore. Invoice logs contain this error:

```plain
BTC: Payment method unavailable (Impossible to create lightning invoice (Object reference not set to an instance of an object.))
```

No exception in the server logs.

### How did you encounter this bug?

Switched the Lightning back-end from local LND to an LNDHub account.

### Relevant log output

```shell
BTC: Payment method unavailable (Impossible to create lightning invoice (Object reference not set to an instance of an object.))
```


### What browser do you use?

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

### Are you sure this is a bug report?

- [X] I confirm this is a bug report
issue_comments:
Just checked and it works for me. 

* Does the Public Node Info page (see the Lightning settings) report the node/connection to be online? 
* What LNDhub backend are you using? It's a known problem that this doesn't work e.g. with the LNbits LNDhub wrapper.

> Does the Public Node Info page (see the Lightning settings) report the node/connection to be online?

Yes. The test connection works (when adding the config), and the public node info page reports it as online.

> What LNDhub backend are you using? It's a known problem that this doesn't work e.g. with the LNbits LNDhub wrapper.

[LndHub.go](https://github.com/getAlby/lndhub.go)

issue_title: [Bug]: exporting invoices from pay.example.com/invoices includes only one store
issue_body:
### What is your BTCPay version?

I'm running BTCPay Server v1.7.3.0+09d5f5a08 (Altcoins).

### How did you deploy BTCPay Server?

I'm running BTCPay Server via Docker.

### What happened?

I have multiple stores configured and tried to export invoices from all stores into one file. I can see the invoices from all stores at `btcpay.example.com/invoices`, but the CSV export contains just invoices from one store.

### How did you encounter this bug?

1. Go to `btcpay.example.com/invoices` (not `btcpay.example.com/stores/0123456789abcdef/invoices`).

issue_title: [Bug]: [Checkout v1] Language dropdown cut off
issue_body:
### What is your BTCPay version?

BTCPay v1.7.2.0



### How did you deploy BTCPay Server?

Docker

### What happened?

The language dropdown is cut off due to some z-index or relative/absolute positioning

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1136761/208196381-b3e5d3d0-7823-48be-91a2-6b1116f8a0e8.png)




### How did you encounter this bug?

Create an invoice and go to checkout page.

### Relevant log output

_No response_

### What browser do you use?

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

### Are you sure this is a bug report?

- [X] I confirm this is a bug report
issue_comments:
The problem is due to the `absolute` positioning of the dropdown container, the page seems to not bother resizing to accommodate an absolutely positioned element when it changes size. 

Changing the dropdown positioning to `relative` allows the dropdown to expand properly, but pushes all the content below it down since its now in normal document flow. This doesn't look too bad imo since only the "Powered by BTCPayServer" element is pushed down - unless you are in a dev environment, since the fake payment stuff is also pushed down and it looks quite weird
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/40777533/209068368-e859f410-7e72-43d8-aa1d-60eba86678fd.png). But I'm not sure if this matters much since it only impacts a dev environment. I can open a PR if someone approves of this solution, otherwise I can look for alternatives.

A better solution may be to make the height of one of the enclosing divs `100vh`. Doing that for either the `modal page` div or the `modal-dialog` div allows the dropdown to expand properly. I've opened a PR for this #4465

@cdmoss cc @dennisreimann Thanks for the fix, unfortunately id did not fix the issue with 1.7.3. It seems that the `min-vh-100` class does not exist with the used Bootstrap version and thus has no effect: https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/v4.0.0/scss/utilities/_sizing.scss

it is there from 4.2.0 onwards though: https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/v4.2.0/scss/utilities/_sizing.scss, maybe we can upgrade or add the class as a custom class ourself?
Yikes, let's apply the style directly then, like @cdmoss did originally. Fixed in #4486.

```

_No response_

issue_comments: Will fix it, thanks for reporting!

Please reopen this issue:

The IPv6 inside and below the QR code needs to be encapsulated by [] or else it is not possible to determine what is still the IP and where the Port number starts:

example is here: https://btcpay.4sats.net/embed/EFHayf8AAHe9hxXhUd4uvzmHsShm6PS2SBvFbg9pDJXq/BTC/ln
It says: ```02c381ab9db7bdad87719252aaa00d3c016855060b77e55a43c8786f9fc40eeec0@2a03:4000:6:b1::2:9735```
but it should be:
```02c381ab9db7bdad87719252aaa00d3c016855060b77e55a43c8786f9fc40eeec0@[2a03:4000:6:b1::2]:9735```

1ml.com shows it correctly.

Thanks.
Probably should be fixed at `NodeInfo.ToString()` level in the library
Checking

@dennisreimann this seems to be cause with the our new store centric approach and somehow we missed the `invoice` filter. I've managed to replicate this. Maybe @bolatovumar wants to take a look?

OK, this problem is gone, but there is an following problem. when i try to push the "Pay invoice" button the _"invoice qr code box"_ doesn't show up. i think invoice and payment request are not connected, because search invoice by payment request also ends in no result.

However, can you see the log for errors which might have occured in the last step of your video, in which you try using the "Pay Invoice" button — this should indeed create a new invoice for that payment request.

you're right, there was a network problem that block my "_invoice box_".

thanx for your help

issue_title: [Bug]: Greenfield API: Update a Webhook bug and undocumented request param
issue_body:
### What is your BTCPay version?

BTCPay v1.6.3.0

### How did you deploy BTCPay Server?

btcpayserver-docker

### What happened?

The ["Update a Webhook"](https://docs.btcpayserver.org/API/Greenfield/v1/#tag/Webhooks/paths/~1api~1v1~1stores~1{storeId}~1webhooks~1{webhookId}/put) endpoint has a major issue causing webhooks to fail. 

1. the request body schema does not mention `secret` (but it works actually if you put it there)
2. if `secret` is not present the secret gets reset or replaced by nothing, not sure

As it is now the update webhook will make your webhooks fail as it seems if the (undocumented) `secret` is not set it resets the passwort.

**Expected behaviour:**  
1. if the `secret` is missing it should not change the webhook secret at all
2. if `secret` should be available to overwrite at all on that endpoint it needs to get documented on API docs

**To discuss**:
- Should secret be allowed to be updated via the update webhook endpoint at all?
- If yes, what happens if the secret is not present in the request, it should imo keep the old secret and not generate a new one because you do not get back the automatically generated secret in the response either
 


### How did you encounter this bug?

see above

### Relevant log output

_No response_

### What browser do you use?

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

### Are you sure this is a bug report?

- [X] I confirm this is a bug report
issue_comments:
Agree that it should not be allowed it at all, however the problem I have now though:

Say I with WooCommerce I want to update the specificEvents with a new release. Assuming this will be fixed in > 1.6.3.0 I need now to send the secret in calling Update Webhook (and also add it in the PHP library) to be backward compatible with releases up to >1.6.3.0 to not have the existing and stored secret get overwritten by the API call. 
So it would be great if the fixed bug release would not throw an error if `secret` is sent but just ignore it silently? Howewer this will be maintenance pain as the param will stop working with >1.6.3.0 on the PHP library too (but needs to be present for all releases before). So this is a bc break even if it was never documented? 😬  

Or we document the param as it works now and change it in GF v2 to not break bc?
Since the GET does not return the secret, I'm inclined to make it so that update ignores the secret

Ok, this means I will add the secret to the PHP lib so I can support updating webhook event subscriptions for all older releases. But starting with an upcoming version this will just silently get ignored. What do we do with the API docs? Just keep them as is without the `secret` param? So I will add a comment to the PHP lib function that this will stop working at some point in case anybody want's to set the secret via update webhook. Sounds good, tbh not many use that endpoint anyway it seems, otherwise there would be more complaints about failing webhook validations 😅

issue_body:
When I create a pull payment for off-chain and the receiver wants to use the LNURL QR feature, the QR does not update to a Bech32 QR when selected unless, the QR window is closed and reopened (sometimes a few times).

This issue actually captured in the feature video on the release notes ([https://blog.btcpayserver.org/btcpay-server-1-6-0/#lnurl-withdraw-support-for-payouts-](https://blog.btcpayserver.org/btcpay-server-1-6-0/#lnurl-withdraw-support-for-payouts-)). issue_title: Crowdfund App Perks with custom price are not taken into account of goal (LNURL method at least)
issue_body:
<!--
Thank you for reporting a technical issue.

This issue tracker is only for bug reports and problems. 

For general questions please read our documentation docs.btcpayserver.org. You can ask technical questions in discussions https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/discussions and general support on our community chat chat.btcpayserver.org

Please fill in as much of the template below as you're able. 
-->

**Describe the bug**

Crowdfund App Perks with custom price are not taken into account of goal (LNURL method at least)

**To Reproduce the bug**
Steps to reproduce the reported bug:
1. Create Crowdfund App
2. Create some Perks with sums
3. Create one Perk with "Price Custom"
4. To go Crowdfund page
5. Click by Custom Perk
6. To pay a Custom Perk by Lightning for example (I didn't test Bitcoin on-chain) by LNURL method
7. When a Custom Perk is paid the sum is not taken into account - a goal volume is not increased and the amount is visible only in the general invoices, but is not visible in the invoices of Crowdfunding itself!

**Expected behavior**

The custom price contribution should be taken into account both in the amount of the goal and visible in invoices of crowdfunding admin area, for example.

<!--
A clear and concise description of what you expected to happen.
-->

**Your BTCPay Environment (please complete the following information):**
- BTCPay Server Version:    1.5.2.0
- Deployment Method:        LunaNode's template
- Browser:                  Firefox 100.*

**Logs (if applicable)**


<!--
Basic logs can be found in Server Settings > Logs. 
More logs https://docs.btcpayserver.org/Troubleshooting/#2-looking-through-the-logs
-->

**Setup Parameters**

<!--
If you're reporting a deployment issue run `. btcpay-setup.sh -i` and paste the setup parameters here with your private information removed or obscured.
-->

**Additional context**

<!--
Add any other context about the problem here.
-->
issue_comments:
LNURL by design doesn't include the amount, after all you did define "custom amount" in the perk. I couldn't replicate the other issue you're reporting, when I pay the LNURL, It is reflected in my crowdfunding.

@Kukks I think we really should ditch lnurl-pay from the checkout page and use the bolt11 zero invoices for zero amount invoices. It just doesn't make sense to use it the way it is set up now.

> LNURL by design doesn't include the amount, after all you did define "custom amount" in the perk. I couldn't replicate the other issue you're reporting, when I pay the LNURL, It is reflected in my crowdfunding.
> 
> @Kukks I think we really should ditch lnurl-pay from the checkout page and use the bolt11 zero invoices for zero amount invoices. It just doesn't make sense to use it the way it is set up now.

I disagree as it disables other features. This is out fo scope for this issue. We can discuss it next week.
What other features? We discussed this already, even fiatjeff said it does not make sense in the invoice checkout. Indeed out of scope, but evidently it causes confusion for people.

> What other features? We discussed this already, even fiatjeff said it does not make sense in the invoice checkout. Indeed out of scope, but evidently it causes confusion for people.

I have not seen that remark by him, curious to see that reasoning. 0 amount invoice support is not very good, only very few wallets support it and I think our handling of different payment amounts to a bolt11  may not even register it in case of over payment.

After all, the problem that I noticed many times was somehow lost behind the topic of zero invoices. I want to say right away - LNURL-pay does not cause me confusion. The problem is different. When I pay Perk with "Price Custom" - it is not added to the amount collected by the Goal. That is, I pay the invoice, but the amount is not counted.

[You can check yourself...](https://pay.fuckedbitcoin.com/apps/3NSbM4hYAqqP1tvZMJFWMJjBqG5t/crowdfund)

UPDATE: I noticed that the Goal sum is not updated only when I click "Custom price" -> LNURL -> showed QR code -> scan ...

If I click "Custom price" -> Standard -> Continue -> Payment method as Lightning LNURL -> scan... - this method increases the Goal sum

That seems to be an issue with RTL, which we are tracking here: https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver-docker/issues/626

Does downgrading RTL help?

issue_title: Improve `store settings` view alignment on mobile [iPhone 8]
issue_body:
Just noticed that the Store Settings view isn't well aligned on iPhone 8 for some reason, would be nice if we could fix this.


https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/36959754/166134812-b7b106da-5614-48c9-bfc0-b7c3f64a3a3b.MOV

issue_title: Pull Payment automatic approval table display error
issue_body: Despite checking "automatically approve", it's displayed incorrectly in the table. Repro:
issue_comments: Did you have payout processors configured? I wonder if this is tied to that? cc @Kukks
Yes I did, just the default 60min configuration.
Looks like whoever implemented this just forgot to set the flag for display. Simple fix in #3738.

issue_title: Wallet Balance balance display error
issue_body: As originally reported by @Bangalisch on Mattermost.

issue_title: Bug when trying to bump fee (CPFP) in 1.4.8 - Can't substract fee from this output because the amount is too small with missing amount XXX
issue_body:
<!--
Thank you for reporting a technical issue.

For general questions please read our documentation docs.btcpayserver.org. You can ask technical questions in discussions https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/discussions and general support on our community chat chat.btcpayserver.org

Please fill in as much of the template below as you're able. 
-->

**Describe the bug**

When trying to bump an uncofirmed transaction I got a white screen of death.

The error is `Can't substract fee from this output because the amount is too small with missing amount 0.00001543`

The error should be shown to the user and not crash.

**Screenshots**

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/36959754/161253757-455917a8-bd14-44f7-9189-327016463b16.mov

- Browser:            Safari 15.00      <!--[e.g. Chrome, Safari]-->
