issue_title: Improve handling of `refunded` invoice in invoices, invoice details and invoice export.
issue_body:
Currently, if an invoice is `refunded`:

- It's not visible in `invoice export` as `refunded` it's `settled` the amount is in +, which can lead to accounting problems.
- In `invoices view` you cannot distinguish refunded invoices, they say `settled` so it's hard for a merchant to pick a particular invoice that may need to be refunded or view a refunded one as they're all `settled`
- In the `invoice details` there's no field indicating an invoice is refunded in any way
- In invoice details there's no indicator of the refunded amount , and you can even click `issue a refund` on an invoice already refunded.

This is an example of an invoice detail of an already refunded invoice.
![Screenshot 2022-03-31 at 16 11 47](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/36959754/161121562-7dc30dc1-d3aa-460a-8c4f-768479807c23.png)
This invoices view shows 3 invoices as settled, all of them `refunded`
![Screenshot 2022-03-31 at 20 08 43](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/36959754/161121685-1332d7f9-4c8d-41fa-b568-f23fe1a8d62a.png)
And most problematic of all, this is an invoice export containing reporting for refunded invoices

![Screenshot 2022-03-31 at 20 10 30](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/36959754/161122013-0cffc780-986e-4f7d-9dc0-8959ee1a32a2.png)

This issue can potentially be broken down into several issues, but for now we'll use it as a tracker

- [ ] Add refunded invoices in invoice export and show refunded amount
- [x] Add refunded `label` in invoices page view to distinguish easier refunded from settled.
- [x] Add indicator that invoice is refunded in invoice details and don't show `issue a refund` button, instead show `preview a refund`


Luckily we log in refunded in database, but we don't show it in the UI, show a fix for all of these shouldn't be that hard as @Kukks pointed out.
I had an idea of introducing `refunded` and `refunded-partial` status, but Kukks was against it.

Thanks to Bas for pointing majority of these problems, I'm just logging an issue.
issue_comments:
Hi @Kukks , was advised that updating the export was the only thing remaining on this ticket. Found a couple items for clarification if you have time:

1. Refunds are list of Pull Payments. However, through the invoice detail page you can only perform a full or partial refund once. Should we anticipate multiple refunds in the future? Seems like we should.
2. If yes to 1, how should we deal with summing multiple refunds that may be in different currencies? Is there a function for this already? Easy solution would be to just take the first one but may cause problems in the future. But I guess we'd have to refactor in multiple areas anyway.
3. `Paid` and `Paid Currency` are strings but `Invoice Due` and `Invoice Price` are numbers. Thoughts? I'll leave as string for now (see draft).

Another separate-but-related issue:
- Export doesn't appear to go off of selected invoices but rather the search bar

@pavlenex

Need a little clarification for desired result -- invoice export actually can have multiple payments per invoice.  Refunds have multiple pull payments per refund and could be added separately like payments. However, as @Kukks  pointed out this is not really an invoice export now.

Some options:

1. Make this a top-level, invoice-only export (no multiple payments or pull payments)
2. Add pull payments, by line like we do for payments
3. Other

Also a separate issue to consider is that exporting invoices does not respect the checkbox selections -- it exports all invoices that have been listed on the page (via filter terms). Will try to join the call!
Update from dev call 7/19: We will go with option 2 -- listing all payments and pull payments
Hey @rustywave are you still up for tackling this one?

@pavlenex, sorry for being MIA! i won't be able to finish this or any others at this time. update is partway done but there are still some clarifications needed on which are the correct fields to use. i can either create a PR or push my changes to a remote branch for the next person to review.

It isn't a bug.
Merchants wants to fix "stuck invoices". Without the button the merchant need to:
* Go in invoice page
* Locate the transaction id
* Go to wallet
* CTRL+F the transaction id (and even that doesn't work if the transaction isn't in the first page)
* Bump it.

This button is meant for merchant to just bump the fee of transactions in an invoice without fuss.
That's more natural, because the reason they bump fee is likely a customer complaining about failed invoice, and this they will be in the invoice list filtering by order id.

issue_title: Filtering for "paid invoices" produces no results
issue_body: **Describe the bug** The `Paid` invoices filter doesn't work anymore. All other invoice filters still work as expected (Invalid, Paid Late, Paid Partial, Paid Over, Unusual, Archived). This worked in all versions up to 1.3.7 and broke in 1.4.0. When filtering for "paid", the filter query will change to `status:processing,status:settled` as defined here: https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/blob/f3592c4782f160a597f845ae5a023bede846e148/BTCPayServer/Views/UIInvoice/ListInvoices.cshtml#L284 Either this code is wrong or the documentation/explanation is, as per here: https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/blob/f3592c4782f160a597f845ae5a023bede846e148/BTCPayServer/Views/UIInvoice/ListInvoices.cshtml#L259 My current workaround is to manually enter `status:complete` which will show all paid (settled) invoices. **To Reproduce the bug** Steps to reproduce the reported bug: 1. Go to 'invoices' 2. Click on 'Paid invoices', via the drop-down menu on the right side of the search button 3. Observe an empty list **Expected behavior** I would expect paid invoices to show up, as they did in previous versions. **Screenshots** (I removed the storeId when taking the screenshots above, but it doesn't matter) **Your BTCPay Environment (please complete the following information):** - BTCPay Server Version: v1.4.3.0 - Deployment Method: Docker - Browser: Firefox
issue_comments: Thanks for reporting this, we no longer have `paid` status[ as of 1.4.0. ](https://blog.btcpayserver.org/btcpay-server-1-4-0/#invoice-states-🧾)Paid is now called `processing` and we should definitely update our UI to reflect that.

For what it's worth, `status:processing` doesn't produce any results either, so this might be a bug in the model or the logic, not purely in the UI.

![Screenshot from 2022-02-08 14-50-31](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/109058/153000517-e6a99f3a-d7ed-4bab-8c7d-e5f3981de04c.png)

The state shown in the list is the new label and it looks like the filter is still applied based on the old status labels. I can reproduce it and filter the invoices using the old labels:

- `paid` (old) -> `processing` (new)
- `completed` (old) -> `settled` (new)
- `confirmed` (old) -> `settled` (new)

Fixing this now.
Fix in #3436, as a workaround `status:paid,status:confirmed,status:complete` can be used.

use `status:paid,status:confirmed,status:complete,status:settled,status:processing` instead.
This is fixed in next release.

issue_title: When target currency in the crowdfunding is BTC, the goal amount is only denominated with up to three decimals and doesn't update
issue_body:
<!--
Thank you for reporting a technical issue.

This issue tracker is only for bug reports and problems. 

For general questions please read our documentation docs.btcpayserver.org. You can ask technical questions in discussions https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/discussions and general support on our community chat chat.btcpayserver.org

Please fill in as much of the template below as you're able. 
-->

**Describe the bug**
As reported on Twitter. When you have `BTC` as a target currency, it's only denominated in two decimals for some reasons, not eight.

This leads into false display of the amount in the crowdfunding app, showing 0.

**To Reproduce the bug**
Steps to reproduce the reported bug:
1. Create a crowdfunding app
2. Put `BTC` as a target currency
3. Make a payment of `0.00001`
4. See error the 0 balance 

**Expected behavior**
The balance should be correctly updated with correct unumber of decimals.
<!--
A clear and concise description of what you expected to happen.
-->

**Screenshots**


<!--
If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.
-->

issue_title: Crash when no payment configured and you click on the `pull payments` or `payouts` in the store.
issue_body:
<!--
Thank you for reporting a technical issue.

This issue tracker is only for bug reports and problems. 

For general questions please read our documentation docs.btcpayserver.org. You can ask technical questions in discussions https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/discussions and general support on our community chat chat.btcpayserver.org

Please fill in as much of the template below as you're able. 
-->

**Describe the bug**
When no payment method is configured yet and you go to the Store > Pull Payments  or Payouts and click on it you'll be greeted with a white screen. 

**To Reproduce the bug**
Steps to reproduce the reported bug:
1. Create a store.
2. Do not configure payment method
3. Click on `pull payments` or `payout` link in the sidebar
4. See error

**Expected behaviour**

If payment method is critical for the operation, we should provide an error once user tries to create a payout or pull payment. Pull payment actually doesn't crash right away, only when you try to create a pull payment without having payment method configured.

<!--
A clear and concise description of what you expected to happen.
-->

**Screenshots**

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/36959754/141455328-a771de69-503f-4b68-bbdb-9d76b4d17b95.mov



<!--
If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.
-->

**Your BTCPay Environment (please complete the following information):** 
- BTCPay Server Version:    <!--[available in the right bottom corner of footer] -->  v1.3.4.0
- Deployment Method:        <!--[e.g. Docker, Manual, Third-Party-host]--> Docker
- Browser:                  <!--[e.g. Chrome, Safari]--> Safari

issue_title: Prevent the creation of below dust limit on-chain payment method for small amount invoices
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
Currently a user can create an invoice for a small amount i.e. 0.10 EUR and get back a qr code for an onchain payment that is below bitcoin's dust limit. Thus no customer could actually pay such an invoice from there wallet.

Imho it would be better to skip the creation of btc-onchain payment method in such cases, so that the invoice view moves on the lightning payment option (if it is available) instantly.

**To Reproduce the bug**
Steps to reproduce the reported bug:
1. create invoice with amout 0.10 EUR
2. try to pay it with your wallet (spoiler: it won't let you)

**Expected behavior**

As described above: I wouldn't allow onchain payment method creation in such cases.

**Your BTCPay Environment (please complete the following information):**
- BTCPay Server Version:    v1.3.2.0 (altcoins)
- Deployment Method:        Docker
- Browser:                  Chrome
issue_comments:
We should just hardcode the limit to 600 satoshi. (this is actually not exact, depending on the wallet type, the dust limit is different, but we shouldn't care too much imho) Bitcoin core and NBX doesn't carry this information.
Best place is in `BitcoinLikePaymentHandler.CreatePaymentMethodDetails`
I can take this one @pavlenex
Do we want to give a proper error when such invoice is created as well?
That would be a good idea. Perhaps we can integrate this into the payment methods criteria flow?
no, this is really a hard limit, shouldn't be configurable.

issue_title: Improve the error message on invoice creation
issue_body:
<!--
Thank you for reporting a technical issue.

This issue tracker is only for bug reports and problems. 

For general questions please read our documentation docs.btcpayserver.org. You can ask technical questions in discussions https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/discussions and general support on our community chat chat.btcpayserver.org

Please fill in as much of the template below as you're able. 
-->

**Describe the bug**
In #2978 we improved the error message, however our UI improvements have now obscured this error message and may lead into confusion on what's really wrong.  Leading to confusions like #3054


The suggested steps are that we combine what we had but also add back in some of the indicators we had in the earlier version, making sure we spit out a proper warning that's contextual.

@NicolasDorier can potentially expand on this as he'd like to address this.
issue_comments:
I have to take some blame for this by bringing up the original cryptic error issue in the first place. 

I agree with what @NicolasDorier said [here](https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/pull/2978#issuecomment-947460305), basically that a cryptic error message that presents usable information is better than a non-cryptic one that doesn't.

In #2978 the cryptic messages were moved to the logs and in #3054 a further check of the logs did yield more actionable information, so at a minimum (if nothing else) the current error message on invoice creation should say something like "Please see logs for more details".

issue_title: Greenfield API: InvoiceExpired event fires twice on partial payment
issue_body:
When you have a partially paid invoice and it expires the `InvoiceExpired` event gets fired twice. Notably first time the property of `partiallyPaid` is `false` (which is wrong) on the second event it is correct and `true`. Likely the partial payment is the cause for this.

I also tested the expiration without a partial payment and there the event fires only once as expected. I also reproduced the problem a second time with a partial payment where it happened again.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1136761/138435446-bc17c14a-4cc0-4474-b523-199bf2787d39.png)

issue_title: Inline JS doesn't work in Safari due to CSP
issue_body:
<!--
Thank you for reporting a technical issue.

This issue tracker is only for bug reports and problems. 

For general questions please read our documentation docs.btcpayserver.org. You can ask technical questions in discussions https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/discussions and general support on our community chat chat.btcpayserver.org

Please fill in as much of the template below as you're able. 
-->

**Describe the bug**

Inline JS doesn't work in Safari and it makes @pavlenex  sad. When you try to interact with an element that has inline JS you see this error in the console:

![Screen Shot 2021-09-29 at 9 26 10 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1934678/135387453-6d522c08-ae61-4996-9fd5-d77548777c8e.png)

To solve this issue we can either relax CSP settings or update the inline JS to be not inline.

We have quite a bit of inline JS throughout:

![Screen Shot 2021-09-29 at 9 27 05 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1934678/135387502-12c10ac4-3f8b-417f-8ef6-7d03b05bd6b0.png)
![Screen Shot 2021-09-29 at 9 27 24 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1934678/135387505-fa4ce554-274e-4ba6-82ff-8d9412ada9e6.png)

**To Reproduce the bug**
Steps to reproduce the reported bug:
1. Go to invoices page in Safari (I'm using Safari 14 but newer version should be fine as well)
2. Click on the checkmark on the top left which is supposed to select all invoices on the page
3. Notice it does nothing
4. Notice there is a CSP error in the console

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1934678/135387716-1116f77f-6435-4d5c-adaa-d3ecebafe7b3.mov

**Expected behavior**

Expect behaviors which rely on JS to work.

**Your BTCPay Environment (please complete the following information):**
- BTCPay Server Version: 1.2.4.0
- Deployment Method: Doesn't matter
- Browser: Safari 14.1.2
issue_comments: Actually I didn't encounter that many issues on Safari 15.0 the only thing that didn't work for me was #2923
No, actually you're right @bolatovumar just tested on latest and safari 15.0 and what you mention is indeed broken.
@bolatovumar They need the `csp-allow` attribute, because recently Nicolas added strict CSP enforcement.

@dennisreimann `csp-allow` doesn't work here, it only works on those elements which use inline JS like this: `href="javascript:console.log('stuff here')"`. This issue happens on inline JS that's used like this `onClick="console.log('stuff here')"`.

I am not sure if this is related issue, but running latest synced from github BTCPayServer v1.2.4.0, one creates Invoice, and in Firefox, Chrome, Brave see blank page on checkout like ".../i/TcyuZbaXJEEVHa8eNNpKPz", in browser console I see many errors like this:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1067032/135918105-6b7e469e-0933-446c-97ad-1768e0ef91cb.png)

result is blank page, I dont see any problems in any logs in btcpayserver docker, I can see source in browser and response code is also 200, so I guess it is problem with JS. Funny enough, in Safari 15 it is working fine.

UPDATE: This is probably caused by using Cloudflare as CDN. It would be great to use BTCPS as before - in my case using CloudFlare as CDN provider.

issue_title: Do not allow invoice `cancellation` for `payment requests` when "Allow payee to create invoices in their own denomination" is not enabled
issue_body:
<!--
Thank you for reporting a technical issue.

This issue tracker is only for bug reports and problems. 

For general questions please read our documentation docs.btcpayserver.org. You can ask technical questions in discussions https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/discussions and general support on our community chat chat.btcpayserver.org

Please fill in as much of the template below as you're able. 
-->

**Describe the bug**

The payment request can be easily canceled, which is a bit out of ordinary behavior because such an invoice is marked as `invalid marked`. If a user is used to seeing `invalid` status in their invoices view, they may make a mistake of labeling it as `complete` thinking the payment request is paid but has not received confirmation on the blockchain on time.

This is indeed an edge-case, but due to this unexpected error, a mistake can happen. I've talked to @Kukks about this and we agreed this shouldn't happen.

The proposal is to disable `payment request cancelation` unless `Allow payee to create invoices in their own denomination` is enabled on the payment request. The reason behind this is to allow payee who may have created a wrong amount to `cancel the invoice`

**To Reproduce the bug**
Steps to reproduce the reported bug:
1. Create a payment request
2. Click `pay`button
3. Click `cancel invoice`
4. Go to `invoices` list and see the payment request invoice marked as `invalid`

![Screenshot 2021-08-23 at 10 43 23](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/36959754/130417757-d85947ad-19fa-4d1e-bff6-34eb8bfe70bb.png)

### Logs

Interestingly enough this also throws an error in logs 

```
021-08-23 08:18:04.491 +00:00 [INF] BTC: New block
2021-08-23 08:18:09.140 +00:00 [INF] User with ID 2c117299-f3e3-424c-8709-587a14c27b95 logged in with 2fa.
2021-08-23 08:30:13.469 +00:00 [ERR] Failed to invoke hub method 'Pay'.
System.NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object.
   at BTCPayServer.PaymentRequest.PaymentRequestHub.Pay(Nullable`1 amount) in /source/BTCPayServer/PaymentRequest/PaymentRequestHub.cs:line 52
   at Microsoft.AspNetCore.SignalR.Internal.DefaultHubDispatcher`1.ExecuteHubMethod(ObjectMethodExecutor methodExecutor, THub hub, Object[] arguments)
   at Microsoft.AspNetCore.SignalR.Internal.DefaultHubDispatcher`1.<>c__DisplayClass13_0.<<Invoke>g__ExecuteInvocation|0>d.MoveNext()
2021-08-23 08:30:22.624 +00:00 [INF] Invoice 9GxoEfPEf8Awf6YSF52qy new event: invoice_created (1001)
2021-08-23 08:30:26.275 +00:00 [INF] Invoice 9GxoEfPEf8Awf6YSF52qy new event: invoice_markedInvalid (1008)
```
issue_comments: I wonder if it would make sense to include an additional status here so that if a user cancels a payment request it's marked as "cancelled" instead of "invalid"? Or would that be too much just for this one case?
Haha please let's not due that, the statuses we have are already mind-boggling for most users. Since this is an edge-case simply not allowing it makes sense to me so concept ack on #2843

issue_comments:
@ndeet looks like the prefix is the wallet root fingerprint. You can see it by navigating to **Store > Settings > Modify** (next to your BTC walet). You will see a row labeled "Root Fingerprint".

I think this explains how it's calculated: https://bitcoin.stackexchange.com/questions/76816/trying-to-understand-parent-fingerprint-in-hd-wallets

> **Describe the bug**
> On setting the `accountKeyPath` data of the [preview proposed store on-chain payment methods](https://docs.btcpayserver.org/API/Greenfield/v1/#operation/StoreOnChainPaymentMethods_GetOnChainPaymentMethodPreview) BTCPay Server shows an unhandled Exception error in the logs.
> 
> **To Reproduce the bug**

> In the API docs the accountKeyPath example seems to have a fingerprint prefixed e.g. `abcd82a1/84'/0'/0'` from what I saw in searching some code it seems to be a fingerprint of the derivationScheme? Not sure how this get computed, any link to read about it + maybe docs need to be more explicit? Nevertheless using the normal account path should also work and if not it should throw a proper error I assume.

The accountKeyPath is a `RootedKeyPath`, which is a combination of a master fingerprint (computed from the seed) and the account path (the path used to generate the xpub from the seed). In BTCPay, we require both of these, and so does the `RootedKeyPath` type.

Ok thanks so the correct way is to hash the private key `ripemd160(sha256(parentpriv))` but only use the first 8 chars as prefix (and combine with the account path)?

The exception when passing `m/84'/0'/0'` still needs fixing and return a proper http error?
Other problem is that this call shouldn't even have a accountKey parameter, we don't use it.
Updated the doc documenting an unused field, and also fixed the underlying issue of not properly reporting when we can't deserialize a JsonException.

issue_title: Refund invoice: rounding error in amount
issue_body:
<!--
Thank you for reporting a technical issue.

This issue tracker is only for bug reports and problems. 

For general questions please read our documentation docs.btcpayserver.org. You can ask technical questions in discussions https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/discussions and general support on our community chat chat.btcpayserver.org

Please fill in as much of the template below as you're able. 
-->

**Describe the bug**
It seems there is a rounding problem in the refund invoice feature. The first amount, should (from my understanding) be the amount received in BTC. (exact amount)

What I get is an offset of 0.00000012 BTC

**To Reproduce the bug**
Steps to reproduce the reported bug:
1. Create an Invoice (requested amount was: 0.00027585 BTC / 10.80 €)
2. have the user pay it partial and let it expire (paid: 0.00023152 ₿)
3. try to issue a refund and check the first option. it will display: 0.00023164 ₿

**Expected behavior**

<!--
A clear and concise description of what you expected to happen.
-->

I would have expected to find the exact amount the user sent to be able to send it back.


<!--
If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.
-->

**Your BTCPay Environment (please complete the following information):**
- BTCPay Server Version:    v1.2.1.0
- Deployment Method:        Docker
- Browser:                  Chrome
issue_comments: There are various options around the refund rate options, can you provide more info?
Seems to be truth, we do some weird rounding on the total, it's never the same as the originally paid one 😱
We need to fix this one and make small release.

issue_title: HTTP Onion Header ignored for local install
issue_body:
I have BTCPay Server installed on a rockpro64.

When opening it via Brave, with local ip or local domain, I don't see the `Open in Tor` option in brave.
This might come from the fact the page is not https.

But this is problematic, because there is no easy way to discover the onion address of your install outside going to server settings / services.

Maybe we should add again the Tor link in the login page...
That said, it seems to happen only if it is opened on HTTP. To make sure we don't get black listed again by google safe browsing, maybe we should show the Tor link on login page only if http.
issue_comments:
Some more context, as I started to look into this a bit and the references might be helpful …

At least from our [general Tor checks](https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/blob/master/BTCPayServer/Hosting/BTCpayMiddleware.cs#L64) it seems we do not exclude the Onion-Location header when using http. 

The [docs](https://community.torproject.org/onion-services/advanced/onion-location/) state this, which explains why the button isn't displayed:

> The webpage defining the Onion-Location header must be served over HTTPS.

So this is indeed the problem and we need to fix it on our end to solve this. Unfortunately we never clearly found out where the [safe browsing issue](https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/issues/2139) came from. Nevertheless the first pass of trying, which removed the Tor link from the login page, did not resolve the issue. So, having the link on the login page might actually solve this one without bringing back the safe browsing problem.
ACK for re-adding the onion link -- may we can just have it in the footer so it is less in your face?

Let's put this on a list for the next design meeting. 
cc @Zaxounette @dstrukt 

I'm not sure where it would be suitable to add it. Once we have dashboard, it can make sense there, but until then, let's brainstorm on where and if we want this re-added.

Personally, I think it should be available on every page to switch easily.

On Sat, 24 Jul 2021, 19:18 Pavlenex, ***@***.***> wrote:

> Let's put this on a list for the next design meeting.
> cc @Zaxounette <https://github.com/Zaxounette> @dstrukt
> <https://github.com/dstrukt>
>
> I'm not sure where it would be suitable to add it. Once we have dashboard,
> it can make sense there, but until then, let's brainstorm on where and if
> we want this re-added.
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you commented.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/issues/2692#issuecomment-886082806>,
> or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AAN357U7FOK5TEIQBVGE4ETTZLYX7ANCNFSM5AEK4BIQ>
> .
>

I think having it available via the footer would be good, but right now we don't display the/a footer on unauthorized pages, which defeats the purpose of e.g. having it available on the login page. 

Maybe we can have a reduced footer there too. I know @dstrukt mentioned he is thinking about tweaking the footer.

Okaay so, there are two types of wallet export in blue wallet, and the one which works is xpub export, the wallet export crashes the page. Regardless, the page shouldn't crash. This is also UX problem, since people may be under impression that it's wallet seed they're importing.

`Account key path` but this is not the case through Bluewallet > show Xpub.

> `Account key path` but this is not the case through Bluewallet > show Xpub.

Blue wallet doesnt export that data, other wallets like Wasabi and hardware wallets like Cobo and Coldcard do.

> In your step1 of reproduce you mean "show wallet XPub" right? Not the export.
> 
> It works for me this way.

This, the other export option from BW is a seed, which we would need more info to import (address format)

issue_title: Automatic app label gives 404
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
When you click on an `i` tooltip for the automatic labels added in #1916 for the `app` it gives 404, because the URL goes to 
apps/xyz instead apps/xyz/pos for example, so app type is not included which leads to 404.


**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Receive a payment through the pos app
2. Go to Wallets and click on the `i` for the app
4. See 404 page

**Expected behavior**
It should open a link from the app.

**Screenshots**
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/36959754/96374872-c6dab580-1175-11eb-9515-4391c144d8d6.png)

**Your BTCPay Environment (please complete the following information):**
- BTCPay Server Version [available in the right bottom corner of footer]  1.0.5.8
- Deployment Method: [e.g. Docker, Manual, Third-Party-hoist]: Docker

**Logs (if applicable)**
Basic logs can be found in Server Settings > Logs. More logs https://docs.btcpayserver.org/Troubleshooting/#2-looking-through-the-logs

**Additional context**
I'm not sure what's expected behavior here. The `x` (remove the label) doesn't work (if the expected behavior is to not remove this then we shouldn't have the `x`.

issue_title: Search by email no longer functions
issue_body:
Searching by an email address alone used to show all invoices with that email address associated with it. However as of last version, this search functionality no longer exists *ON NEW ORDERS* only. 

Instead any email search simply returns "There are no invoices matching your criteria."

Example:
Searching for my email shows all results from a few months ago

Searching a new customers email address who only placed orders today shows "There are no invoices matching your criteria."

issue_title: Checkout URL incompatible with .onion
issue_body:
Having issue with .onion address for website. 
User loads a cart in tor browser and proceeds to checkout then our btcpayserver is selected. 
URL loads with invoice?id=
User selects the .onion redirect and 404 error is produced. 
When manually pulling the invoice up from the server side it uses a URL with /i/ in place of above. 
Copying this into tor brower and selecting .onion correctly displays the invoice.
issue_comments:
https://btcpay.minefarmbuy.com/invoice?id=4DN7mQDDRKRF9PQE7EV2aP 
This is link the website produces. - .onion 404's
vs.

https://btcpay.minefarmbuy.com/i/4DN7mQDDRKRF9PQE7EV2aP
This is the link btcpayserver produces via invoices tab. - .onion loads

Wondering if there is a plugin issue with the permalink store side that conflicts with the .onion
I see the onion header is not set correctly: `http://juz3qfqadb6pi5qpbajmbi3rplybzvvwvu7eutea7ic5gh5yaav6gaid.onion/invoice`
Please use the issue template next time. You did not provide enough info for me to debug and work with this way.

issue_title: number of notifications in top bar is not updated after deleting the notifications from UI
issue_body:
**Describe the problem/bug**
After deleting the "notifications", the "bell" icon on the right of the top bar still shows that we have notifications to read.

Steps to reproduce:
 - click on the "bell" icon on the top right (https://btcpay.cryptorefills.com/Notifications/Index)
 - select "500" to show 500 notifications per page
 - select all the notifications and "delete" them (without marking as "read")
 - the top bar shown a wrong number

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2782202/89542603-2cb6d280-d800-11ea-97cf-4a8c595a4581.png)


**Your environment**
* Version of BTCPay Server: 1.0.5.4
* Deployment method: Docker


**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. click on the "bell" icon on the top right (https://btcpay.cryptorefills.com/Notifications/Index)
2. select "500" to show 500 notifications per page
3. select all the notifications and "delete" them
4. the top bar shown a wrong number


**Expected behavior**
The "bell" icon should show no notifications to read.

**Actual behavior**
The "bell" icon still show notifications to read.
issue_comments:
This is actually a different cause... The bottom "Page Size" selector is wrong in what it brings up. 

For example, If you select 500, as you did, there is only 250 results presented. So what is happening is that you are expecting to clear 500 but only 250 are being cleared.

@astupidmoose just looking at the code now and it doesn't look like the pagination is wrong.

@simonlucalandi does the notification number by the bell icon go down when you "delete" notifications? Or does it just stay the same?

> @astupidmoose just looking at the code now and it doesn't look like the pagination is wrong.

When I navigate to /Notifications/Index?skip=0&count=50 I get the following: 
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3916318/89606458-db6d0a00-d824-11ea-9663-d3e0736b7251.png)

I always get exactly half of the results the pagination has been asked to provide. 
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3916318/89606503-fe97b980-d824-11ea-914a-33e0edc8beae.png)
@astupidmoose ok, weird. I'm trying to repro it but it's not easy as I don't have any notifications and I'm trying to trigger some now somehow.

> 
> 
> @astupidmoose just looking at the code now and it doesn't look like the pagination is wrong.
> 
> @simonlucalandi does the notification number by the bell icon go down when you "delete" notifications? Or does it just stay the same?

The numbers goes down, but of a wrong amount.

issue_title: Crowdfund app doesn't count old invoices
issue_body:
**Describe the problem/bug**
If you click `Count all invoices created on the store as part of the crowdfunding goal` under `Crowdfund` app, it doesn't count old invoices.
It counts only invoices created after enabling the option.

**Your environment**
* Version of BTCPay Server: v1.0.5.2

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Create a Crowdfund app

It should count old invoices,

issue_title: RTL QR Code Error: code length overflow
issue_body:
**Describe the problem/bug**

**Your environment**

issue_title: [Crowdfund app] Raised amount tooltip shows wrong amount if app is raising in fiat
issue_body:
**Describe the problem/bug**
If your crowdfund app specifies amount to raise in fiat then your raised amounts tooltip shows amount rounded to two decimal points instead of eight.

**Your environment**
* Version of BTCPay Server: v1.0.3.131 

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Create a crowdfund app
2. Specify an amount to raise in USD or any other fiat option
3. Contribute some amount in crypto with more than two decimal points in amount (something like 0.1192345)
4. See that the amount raised will be shown as 0.12 in the tooltip

The issue is even worse if you are contributing a smaller amount like 0.001245. You will get 0.00 in the tooltip.

**Expected behavior**
Tooltip always shows contributed amounts in correct divisibility amounts.

**Screenshots/Links**
See screenshot below:
![Screen Shot 2019-09-15 at 12 23 15 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1934678/64926627-86f81080-d7b4-11e9-8ae0-176f67e30521.png)

**Additional context**
This issue was introduced by me earlier in a [PR](https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/pull/987) that fixed issue #926.

The issue was that I assumed `srvModel.currencyData.divisibility` always equals divisibility number for crypto amounts (so 8) but it's equal to 2 if you are raising in fiat. I guess the raised amount number might have to be formatted server-side instead.

issue_title: Make ASCII non-alphanumerical characters valid in a POS item description
issue_body:
**_Describe the problem/bug_**
In the PoS app, when editing the text description of a shopping item, it seems the character inputs can only be alphanumerical.

**_Your environment_**
* _Version of BTCPay Server:_ V1.0.3.120
* _Deployment method:_ Lunanode


**_To Reproduce_**
_Steps to reproduce the behavior:_
1. Create a POS app
2. Edit a shopping item
3. Try to input any ASCII non-alphanumerical character (#&@%µ£$§...)
4. Submit changes and get thrown back to general POS app settings (error) instead of POS app list (success).

**_Additional context_**
I have not tested every ASCII non-alphanumerical character, but as an example, I wanted to put a hashtag (#) for a shopping item in the description.
Without the hashtag the submission goes thought, with it, it fails.
issue_comments: Asp.net defends against some characters to stop cross script attacks. It’s most likely that the error is because of that.  Do you have an error message?

> Asp.net defends against some characters to stop cross script attacks. It’s most likely that the error is because of that. Do you have an error message?

I might have to rewrite this PR.

1°) The error "Invalid template" appears in fact when I have a backspace in the description (to separate sentences, for instance).

2°) On the other hand, when inputting for example a hashtag + a word ( #example ), update goes through, BUT hashtag does not appear in the UI viewer side.
On top of that, if put in the middle of the description, the hashtag makes the following characters appear blank in the UI viewer side.

No error ever appears on this second part.

@Zaxounette the issue seems to be specifically with # and & characters. If you input # character then nothing after this character shows up (so in #&@%µ£$§🤣 nothing will show up). If you input & character then you get & (so &@%µ£$§🤣 --> &@%µ£$§🤣). If you don't use any of these characters then it seems to be fine.

@Zaxounette unfortunately the above workaround doesn't work if you just edit the description by clicking on the "Edit" button and then save as their is client-side character escaping that happens once you click "Save". I'm not sure if this client-side escaping is needed as there is backend escaping that must be happening. I hope someone else can confirm this.

issue_title: Payment request with "Allow payee to create invoices" marked, allow to put 0 as payment amount
issue_body:
**Describe the problem/bug**
See title

**Your environment**
BTCPayServer v1.0.3.94

**To Reproduce**
Create payment request with Allow payee to create invoices in their own denomination checked. Try to make payment fo 0.

**Expected behavior**
Validate if payment amount > 0

**Actual behavior**
Payment request for 0 is created.
issue_comments:
Hi @rvrv 

If you set the payment request value to $0, your request will not produce invoices to accept payments, See [Example1](https://btcps0.com/payment-requests/737387dc-af42-409f-bd6d-d2725ee840f5). 

If you set the payment request value above $0, users can make a "payment denomination of $0" because btcpay accepts over payment of invoices. See [Example2](https://btcps0.com/payment-requests/d7554462-e16b-4169-81f5-831becc3b2d9)

You can also modify the POS system to accept payments of any denomination. The link can be shared just like the payment request link. See the [modified Web POS](https://blog.btcpayserver.org/how-to-accept-bitcoin-donations/) section of this blog. See [Example3](https://btcps0.com/apps/3azQ7EFLKjsxaheTSs1wDuYE4oLE/pos)

Does that help with your issue? If not, please explain more about your issue :)

Hi @britttttk 
Thanks a lot for your swift response.

> If you set the payment request value above $0, users can make a "payment denomination of $0" because btcpay accepts over payment of invoices. See [Example2](https://btcps0.com/payment-requests/d7554462-e16b-4169-81f5-831becc3b2d9)

I don't quite understand why this could be the case to allow user to create $0 invoice. Could you elaborate a bit? 

Furthermore i don't see an option to 'Cancel current invoice' in case of payment requests created with my instance of btcpay. This feature is quite new or i missed something?

Thanks again.

> option to 'Cancel current invoice'

Yes please update your server by going to: Server Settings > Maintenance > Update
The cancel feature for payment requests was introduced in a recent version. 

> the case to allow user to create $0 invoice

I think because btcpay is able to produce an address for each invoice request regardless of amount, so it's allowed. There is probably someone else who knows the exact reason for this. 

@rvrv happy to help :)

There's no real use case here. It's just extra work to validate against it and nothing bad happens if you create an invoice of 0 amount since you can cancel it now. I'll look into adding a validation check. :)

issue_title: Store Email Password not saving
issue_body: On the email settings for stores, the password does not save after clicking "Save". The server SMTP settings save all fields, and email works. For the store however, the password field is blank after saving and sending email generates a login error for the smtp server. The only way to send a test email for the store email settings is to enter the password and then click "test".
issue_comments: Thanks for reporting, this is indeed a bug that was fixed in [0b98e ](https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/pull/642/commits/0b98e362d5c9e5f16898f214276e03c1060fcef0)for email settings on a Server level but has not been done for the store-level e-mails.
Thanks. I haven't been able to find any documentation on this; what is the purpose of the store-level email settings? In what scenario will store-level emails be sent?

Not many stuff at this point, you can configure certain apps like crowdfunding, payment button or payment requests and receive emails (@Kukks on which actions will those be sent? Am I right on this one?). 

Should you need crazy complex email setup, I advise you to give our[ BTC Transmuter a try](https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcTransmuter), it's still in alpha though and may require a bit of tech knowledge to get started.

We'll document it better once everything is tied up neatly.

Yup, if `fullNotifications` or `extendedNotifications` are enabled on an invoice along with having the `notificationEmail` set, you will receive an email with some json data on each status update and on each payment

Got it. Maybe it's the wrong place for questions, but what is the purpose of "redirect invoice to redirect url automatically after paid"? If not enabled, is there a button for the user to be manually taken back to the redirect url after payment?
